El enfoque superó significativamente los modelos de referencia en métricas de rendimiento total, ratio de Sharpe y retracción máxima, demostrando el valor de combinar múltiples métodos analíticos.

title: Regularized Latent Dynamics Prediction is a Strong Baseline For Behavioral Foundation Models
summary: Regularized Latent Dynamics Prediction (RLDP) uses orthogonality regularization to maintain feature diversity in zero-shot reinforcement learning, matching or surpassing complex representation learning methods. RLDP performs particularly well in low-coverage data scenarios where prior approaches typically struggle.
content:
arXiv:2603.15857v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Behavioral Foundation Models (BFMs) produce agents with the capability to adapt to any unknown reward or task. These methods, however, are only able to produce near-optimal policies for the reward functions that are in the span of some pre-existing state features, making the choice of state features crucial to the expressivity of the BFM. As a result, BFMs are trained using a variety of complex objectives and require sufficient dataset coverage, to train task-useful spanning features. In this work, we examine the question: are these complex representation learning objectives necessary for zero-shot RL? Specifically, we revisit the objective of self-supervised next-state prediction in latent space for state feature learning, but observe that such an objective alone is prone to increasing state-feature similarity, and subsequently reducing span. We propose an approach, Regularized Latent Dynamics Prediction (RLDP), that adds a simple orthogonality regularization to maintain feature diversity and can match or surpass state-of-the-art complex representation learning methods for zero-shot RL. Furthermore, we empirically show that prior approaches perform poorly in low-coverage scenarios where RLDP still succeeds.

zh.title: 正则化潜在动力预测是行为基础模型的强大基准
zh.summary: 正则化潜在动力预测（RLDP）使用正交性正则化来维持零射强化学习中的特征多样性，匹配或超过复杂的表示学习方法。RLDP在低覆盖数据场景中表现特别好，而先前的方法通常在这些场景中苦苦挣扎。

fr.title: La prédiction de la dynamique latente régularisée est une base solide pour les modèles de fondation comportementaux
fr.summary: La prédiction de la dynamique latente régularisée (RLDP) utilise la régularisation de l'orthogonalité pour maintenir la diversité des caractéristiques dans l'apprentissage par renforcement sans exemple, égalant ou surpassant les méthodes complexes d'apprentissage des représentations. RLDP fonctionne particulièrement bien dans les scénarios de données à faible couverture où les approches antérieures peinent généralement.

de.title: Regularisierte Latent-Dynamik-Vorhersage ist eine starke Grundlage für Verhaltensgrundmodelle
de.summary: Regularisierte Latent-Dynamik-Vorhersage (RLDP) verwendet Orthogonalitätsregularisierung, um die Merkmalsvielfalt beim Zero-Shot-Reinforcement-Learning zu bewahren und entspricht oder übertrifft komplexe Representationslernmethoden. RLDP funktioniert besonders gut in Szenarien mit niedriger Datenfülle, wo frühere Ansätze typischerweise schwierig sind.

es.title: La predicción de dinámica latente regularizada es una base sólida para modelos de fundación de comportamiento
es.summary: La predicción de dinámica latente regularizada (RLDP) utiliza regularización de ortogonalidad para mantener la diversidad de características en el aprendizaje por refuerzo sin ejemplo, igualando o superando métodos complejos de aprendizaje de representación. RLDP funciona particularmente bien en escenarios de datos de baja cobertura donde los enfoques anteriores típicamente luchan.

title: Resilience Meets Autonomy: Governing Embodied AI in Critical Infrastructure
summary: Embodied AI systems in critical infrastructure require bounded autonomy within hybrid governance architectures to handle cascading failures beyond their training assumptions. The paper outlines oversight modes mapped to different sectors based on task complexity and risk, drawing on EU AI Act and ISO safety standards.
content:
arXiv:2603.15885v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Critical infrastructure increasingly incorporates embodied AI for monitoring, predictive maintenance, and decision support. However, AI systems designed to handle statistically representable uncertainty struggle with cascading failures and crisis dynamics that exceed their training assumptions. This paper argues that Embodied AIs resilience depends on bounded autonomy within a hybrid governance architecture. We outline four oversight modes and map them to critical infrastructure sectors based on task complexity, risk level, and consequence severity. Drawing on the EU AI Act, ISO safety standards, and crisis management research, we argue that effective governance requires a structured allocation of machine capability and human judgement.

zh.title: 韧性与自主性的碰撞：治理关键基础设施中的具身AI
zh.summary: 关键基础设施中的具身AI系统需要在混合治理架构内实现有限自主性，以应对超出其训练假设的级联故障。该论文概述了根据任务复杂性和风险映射到不同部门的监督模式，借鉴了欧盟AI法案和ISO安全标准。

fr.title: Résilience et autonomie : gouverner l'IA incarnée dans les infrastructures critiques
fr.summary: Les systèmes d'IA incarnée dans les infrastructures critiques nécessitent une autonomie bornée au sein d'architectures de gouvernance hybrides pour gérer les défaillances en cascade au-delà de leurs hypothèses de formation. L'article expose les modes de surveillance cartographiés aux différents secteurs en fonction de la complexité des tâches et du risque, en s'appuyant sur la loi sur l'IA de l'UE et les normes de sécurité ISO.

de.title: Resilienz trifft Autonomie: Governance verkörperter KI in kritischer Infrastruktur
de.summary: Verkörperte KI-Systeme in kritischen Infrastrukturen erfordern begrenzte Autonomie innerhalb hybrider Governance-Architekturen, um kaskadierende Ausfälle jenseits ihrer Trainingsannahmen zu bewältigen. Das Papier beschreibt Aufsichtsmodi, die auf verschiedene Sektoren basierend auf Aufgabenkomplexität und Risiken abgebildet werden, unter Bezugnahme auf das EU-KI-Gesetz und ISO-Sicherheitsstandards.

es.title: Resiliencia Encuentra Autonomía: Gobernando la IA Encarnada en Infraestructura Crítica
es.summary: Los sistemas de IA encarnada en infraestructura crítica requieren autonomía limitada dentro de arquitecturas de gobernanza híbrida para manejar fallos en cascada más allá de sus suposiciones de entrenamiento. El documento describe modos de supervisión mapeados a diferentes sectores basados en complejidad de tareas y riesgo, basándose en la Ley de IA de la UE y estándares de seguridad ISO.

title: AsgardBench - Evaluating Visually Grounded Interactive Planning Under Minimal Feedback
summary: AsgardBench evaluates interactive planning in embodied AI by having agents adapt plans based on visual observations without rich corrective feedback. Evaluations reveal that leading vision language models struggle with visual grounding and state tracking, highlighting gaps in using visual information for plan adaptation.
content:
arXiv:2603.15888v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: With AsgardBench we aim to evaluate visually grounded, high-level action sequence generation and interactive planning, focusing specifically on plan adaptation during execution based on visual observations rather than navigation or low-level manipulation. In the landscape of embodied AI benchmarks, AsgardBench targets the capability category of interactive planning, which is more sophisticated than offline high-level planning as it requires agents to revise plans in response to environmental feedback, yet remains distinct from low-level execution.
  Unlike prior embodied AI benchmarks that conflate reasoning with navigation or provide rich corrective feedback that substitutes for perception, AsgardBench restricts agent input to images, action history, and lightweight success/failure signals, isolating interactive planning in a controlled simulator without low-level control noise.
  The benchmark contains 108 task instances spanning 12 task types, each systematically varied through object state, placement, and scene configuration. These controlled variations create conditional branches in which a single instruction can require different action sequences depending on what the agent observes, emphasizing conditional branching and plan repair during execution.
  Our evaluations of leading vision language models show that performance drops sharply without visual input, revealing weaknesses in visual grounding and state tracking that ultimately undermine interactive planning. Our benchmark zeroes in on a narrower question: can a model actually use what it sees to adapt a plan when things do not go as expected?

zh.title: AsgardBench - 在最小反馈下评估视觉基础交互规划
zh.summary: AsgardBench通过让代理在没有丰富更正反馈的情况下根据视觉观察调整计划来评估具身AI中的交互规划。评估显示，领先的视觉语言模型在视觉基础定位和状态跟踪方面存在困难，突出了在计划适应中使用视觉信息的差距。

fr.title: AsgardBench - Évaluation de la Planification Interactive Visuellement Ancrée sous Retour Minimal
fr.summary: AsgardBench évalue la planification interactive en IA incarnée en permettant aux agents d'adapter les plans sur la base d'observations visuelles sans rétroaction corrective riche. Les évaluations révèlent que les principaux modèles de langage visuel ont du mal à l'ancrage visuel et au suivi des états, mettant en évidence les lacunes dans l'utilisation des informations visuelles pour l'adaptation des plans.

de.title: AsgardBench - Bewertung visuell verankerter interaktiver Planung unter minimalem Feedback
de.summary: AsgardBench bewertet interaktive Planung in verkörperter KI, indem sie Agenten ermöglicht, Pläne basierend auf visuellen Beobachtungen ohne umfassendes Korrektivfeedback anzupassen. Bewertungen zeigen, dass führende Vision-Language-Modelle bei visueller Verankerung und Zustandsverfolgung Schwierigkeiten haben, was auf Lücken bei der Verwendung visueller Informationen zur Plananpassung hindeutet.

es.title: AsgardBench - Evaluación de Planificación Interactiva Visualmente Fundamentada bajo Retroalimentación Mínima
es.summary: AsgardBench evalúa la planificación interactiva en IA encarnada permitiendo que los agentes adapten planes basados en observaciones visuales sin retroalimentación correctiva rica. Las evaluaciones revelan que los principales modelos de lenguaje visual tienen dificultades con la fundamentación visual y el seguimiento de estado, destacando las brechas en el uso de información visual para la adaptación de planes.

title: Prompt Engineering for Scale Development in Generative Psychometrics
summary: Adaptive prompting strategies substantially outperform non-adaptive approaches in generating personality assessment items with large language models, reducing semantic redundancy and improving structural validity. The benefits scale with model capability, demonstrating the critical importance of prompt-model interactions in psychometric pipelines.
content:
arXiv:2603.15909v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This Monte Carlo simulation examines how prompt engineering strategies shape the quality of large language model (LLM)--generated personality assessment items within the AI-GENIE framework for generative psychometrics. Item pools targeting the Big Five traits were generated using multiple prompting designs (zero-shot, few-shot, persona-based, and adaptive), model temperatures, and LLMs, then evaluated and reduced using network psychometric methods. Across all conditions, AI-GENIE reliably improved structural validity following reduction, with the magnitude of its incremental contribution inversely related to the quality of the incoming item pool. Prompt design exerted a substantial influence on both pre- and post-reduction item quality. Adaptive prompting consistently outperformed non-adaptive strategies by sharply reducing semantic redundancy, elevating pre-reduction structural validity, and preserving substantially larger item pool, particularly when paired with newer, higher-capacity models. These gains were robust across temperature settings for most models, indicating that adaptive prompting mitigates common trade-offs between creativity and psychometric coherence. An exception was observed for the GPT-4o model at high temperatures, suggesting model-specific sensitivity to adaptive constraints at elevated stochasticity. Overall, the findings demonstrate that adaptive prompting is the strongest approach in this context, and that its benefits scale with model capability, motivating continued investigation of model--prompt interactions in generative psychometric pipelines.

zh.title: 生成心理测量学中的提示工程规模开发
zh.summary: 自适应提示策略在使用大型语言模型生成人格评估项目方面明显优于非自适应方法，减少语义冗余并改进结构效度。收益随模型能力而增加，显示了提示-模型交互在心理测量管道中的关键重要性。

fr.title: Ingénierie des Invites pour le Développement d'Échelle en Psychométrie Générative
fr.summary: Les stratégies d'invitation adaptative surpassent considérablement les approches non adaptatives dans la génération d'éléments d'évaluation de la personnalité avec de grands modèles de langage, réduisant la redondance sémantique et améliorant la validité structurelle. Les avantages se multiplient avec la capacité du modèle, démontrant l'importance critique des interactions invite-modèle dans les pipelines psychométriques.

de.title: Prompt-Engineering für Skalenentwicklung in generativer Psychometrie
de.summary: Adaptive Prompting-Strategien übertreffen nicht-adaptive Ansätze erheblich bei der Generierung von Persönlichkeitsbewertungselementen mit großen Sprachmodellen, reduzieren semantische Redundanz und verbessern die strukturelle Validität. Die Vorteile skalieren mit der Modellkapazität und demonstrieren die kritische Bedeutung von Prompt-Modell-Interaktionen in psychometrischen Pipelines.

es.title: Ingeniería de Prompts para Desarrollo de Escala en Psicometría Generativa
es.summary: Las estrategias de prompting adaptativas superan sustancialmente los enfoques no adaptativos en la generación de elementos de evaluación de personalidad con modelos de lenguaje grande, reduciendo la redundancia semántica y mejorando la validez estructural. Los beneficios se escalan con la capacidad del modelo, demostrando la importancia crítica de las interacciones prompt-modelo en canales psicométricos.

title: Semi-Autonomous Formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau Equilibrium
summary: Researchers completed a Lean 4 formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau equilibrium using AI-assisted tools including Gemini DeepThink for proof generation and Claude Code for translation. The project demonstrates AI's potential in mathematical research, completing a formal proof in 10 days with minimal human supervision.
content:
arXiv:2603.15929v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a complete Lean 4 formalization of the equilibrium characterization in the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau (VML) system, which describes the motion of charged plasma. The project demonstrates the full AI-assisted mathematical research loop: an AI reasoning model (Gemini DeepThink) generated the proof from a conjecture, an agentic coding tool (Claude Code) translated it into Lean from natural-language prompts, a specialized prover (Aristotle) closed 111 lemmas, and the Lean kernel verified the result. A single mathematician supervised the process over 10 days at a cost of \$200, writing zero lines of code.
  The entire development process is public: all 229 human prompts, and 213 git commits are archived in the repository. We report detailed lessons on AI failure modes -- hypothesis creep, definition-alignment bugs, agent avoidance behaviors -- and on what worked: the abstract/concrete proof split, adversarial self-review, and the critical role of human review of key definitions and theorem statements. Notably, the formalization was completed before the final draft of the corresponding math paper was finished.

zh.title: 半自主形式化弗拉索夫-麦克斯韦-朗道平衡
zh.summary: 研究人员完成了使用AI辅助工具（包括用于证明生成的Gemini DeepThink和用于翻译的Claude Code）的Lean 4弗拉索夫-麦克斯韦-朗道平衡形式化。该项目展示了AI在数学研究中的潜力，在最少人工监督的情况下在10天内完成了形式化证明。

fr.title: Formalisation Semi-Autonome de l'Équilibre de Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont complété une formalisation Lean 4 de l'équilibre de Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau en utilisant des outils assistés par l'IA, notamment Gemini DeepThink pour la génération de preuves et Claude Code pour la traduction. Le projet démontre le potentiel de l'IA dans la recherche mathématique, en complétant une preuve formelle en 10 jours avec une supervision humaine minimale.

de.title: Semi-autonome Formalisierung des Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau-Gleichgewichts
de.summary: Forscher vollendeten eine Lean-4-Formalisierung des Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau-Gleichgewichts mit KI-gestützten Tools, einschließlich Gemini DeepThink für die Beweiserstellung und Claude Code für die Übersetzung. Das Projekt zeigt das Potenzial von KI in der mathematischen Forschung, indem es einen formalen Beweis in 10 Tagen mit minimaler menschlicher Aufsicht vervollständigte.

es.title: Formalización Semiautónoma del Equilibrio de Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau
es.summary: Los investigadores completaron una formalización en Lean 4 del equilibrio de Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau utilizando herramientas asistidas por IA, incluyendo Gemini DeepThink para la generación de pruebas y Claude Code para la traducción. El proyecto demuestra el potencial de la IA en la investigación matemática, completando una prueba formal en 10 días con una supervisión humana mínima.

title: Argumentative Human-AI Decision-Making: Toward AI Agents That Reason With Us, Not For Us
summary: Argumentative Human-AI Decision-Making combines computational argumentation with LLMs to create transparent, contestable reasoning where decisions are revisable rather than fixed. This approach enables AI agents to engage in dialectical processes with humans, moving from making decisions for people to reasoning with them transparently.
content:
arXiv:2603.15946v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Computational argumentation offers formal frameworks for transparent, verifiable reasoning but has traditionally been limited by its reliance on domain-specific information and extensive feature engineering. In contrast, LLMs excel at processing unstructured text, yet their opaque nature makes their reasoning difficult to evaluate and trust. We argue that the convergence of these fields will lay the foundation for a new paradigm: Argumentative Human-AI Decision-Making. We analyze how the synergy of argumentation framework mining, argumentation framework synthesis, and argumentative reasoning enables agents that do not just justify decisions, but engage in dialectical processes where decisions are contestable and revisable -- reasoning with humans rather than for them. This convergence of computational argumentation and LLMs is essential for human-aware, trustworthy AI in high-stakes domains.

zh.title: 论证性人类-AI决策：与我们一起推理的AI代理，而非替我们决策
zh.summary: 论证性人类-AI决策将计算论证与大语言模型相结合，创造透明的、可争议的推理，其中决策是可修订的而非固定的。这种方法使AI代理能够与人类进行辩证过程，从替人类做决策转变为与人类透明地推理。

fr.title: Prise de Décision Argumentative Homme-IA: Vers des Agents IA qui Raisonnent Avec Nous, Pas Pour Nous
fr.summary: La prise de décision argumentative homme-IA combine l'argumentation computationnelle avec les LLM pour créer un raisonnement transparent et contestable où les décisions sont révisables plutôt que fixes. Cette approche permet aux agents IA de s'engager dans des processus dialectiques avec les humains, passant de la prise de décisions pour les gens à un raisonnement transparent avec eux.

de.title: Argumentative Mensch-KI-Entscheidungsfindung: Hin zu KI-Agenten, die Mit Uns Denken, Nicht Für Uns
de.summary: Argumentative Mensch-KI-Entscheidungsfindung verbindet rechnergestützte Argumentation mit LLMs, um transparentes, anfechtbares Denken zu schaffen, bei dem Entscheidungen revidierbar statt fest sind. Dieser Ansatz ermöglicht es KI-Agenten, sich an dialektischen Prozessen mit Menschen zu beteiligen und von der Entscheidungsfindung für Menschen zu transparentem Denken mit ihnen zu wechseln.

es.title: Toma de Decisiones Argumentativa Humano-IA: Hacia Agentes de IA que Razonen Con Nosotros, No Por Nosotros
es.summary: La toma de decisiones argumentativa humano-IA combina la argumentación computacional con LLM para crear un razonamiento transparente y cuestionable donde las decisiones son revisables en lugar de fijas. Este enfoque permite que los agentes de IA se involucren en procesos dialécticos con los humanos, pasando de tomar decisiones por las personas a razonar con ellas de manera transparente.

title: Protein Design with Agent Rosetta: A Case Study for Specialized Scientific Agents
summary: Agent Rosetta combines large language models with Rosetta protein design software, enabling design of both canonical amino acids and non-canonical building blocks where specialized ML methods fail. The work demonstrates that environment design is essential for successfully integrating LLM agents with specialized scientific software.
content:
arXiv:2603.15952v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are capable of emulating reasoning and using tools, creating opportunities for autonomous agents that execute complex scientific tasks. Protein design provides a natural testbed: although machine learning (ML) methods achieve strong results, these are largely restricted to canonical amino acids and narrow objectives, leaving unfilled need for a generalist tool for broad design pipelines. We introduce Agent Rosetta, an LLM agent paired with a structured environment for operating Rosetta, the leading physics-based heteropolymer design software, capable of modeling non-canonical building blocks and geometries. Agent Rosetta iteratively refines designs to achieve user-defined objectives, combining LLM reasoning with Rosetta's generality. We evaluate Agent Rosetta on design with canonical amino acids, matching specialized models and expert baselines, and with non-canonical residues -- where ML approaches fail -- achieving comparable performance. Critically, prompt engineering alone often fails to generate Rosetta actions, demonstrating that environment design is essential for integrating LLM agents with specialized software. Our results show that properly designed environments enable LLM agents to make scientific software accessible while matching specialized tools and human experts.

zh.title: 蛋白质设计与Agent Rosetta：专业科学代理的案例研究
zh.summary: Agent Rosetta将大语言模型与Rosetta蛋白质设计软件相结合，能够设计规范氨基酸和非规范构建块，其中专门的机器学习方法失效。这项工作表明，环境设计对于成功将LLM代理与专业科学软件集成至关重要。

fr.title: Conception de Protéines avec Agent Rosetta: Une Étude de Cas pour les Agents Scientifiques Spécialisés
fr.summary: Agent Rosetta combine les grands modèles de langage avec le logiciel de conception de protéines Rosetta, permettant la conception d'acides aminés canoniques et de blocs de construction non canoniques où les méthodes d'apprentissage automatique spécialisées échouent. Ce travail démontre que la conception de l'environnement est essentielle pour intégrer avec succès les agents LLM avec des logiciels scientifiques spécialisés.

de.title: Proteindesign mit Agent Rosetta: Eine Fallstudie für spezialisierte wissenschaftliche Agenten
de.summary: Agent Rosetta verbindet große Sprachmodelle mit der Rosetta-Proteindesign-Software und ermöglicht das Design sowohl kanonischer Aminosäuren als auch nicht-kanonischer Bausteine, bei denen spezialisierte ML-Methoden fehlschlagen. Die Arbeit zeigt, dass Umgebungsdesign essentiell ist, um LLM-Agenten erfolgreich mit spezialisierter wissenschaftlicher Software zu integrieren.

es.title: Diseño de Proteínas con Agent Rosetta: Un Estudio de Caso para Agentes Científicos Especializados
es.summary: Agent Rosetta combina grandes modelos de lenguaje con software de diseño de proteínas Rosetta, permitiendo el diseño de aminoácidos canónicos y bloques de construcción no canónicos donde fallan los métodos especializados de aprendizaje automático. El trabajo demuestra que el diseño del entorno es esencial para integrar exitosamente agentes LLM con software científico especializado.

title: Optimizing Hospital Capacity During Pandemics: A Dual-Component Framework for Strategic Patient Relocation
summary: This research proposes a two-part framework combining time series prediction and simulation modeling to optimize hospital capacity during pandemics through strategic patient relocation, helping administrators anticipate demand and distribute resources efficiently.
content:
arXiv:2603.15960v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has placed immense strain on hospital systems worldwide, leading to critical capacity challenges. This research proposes a two-part framework to optimize hospital capacity through patient relocation strategies. The first component involves developing a time series prediction model to forecast patient arrival rates. Using historical data on COVID-19 cases and hospitalizations, the model will generate accurate forecasts of future patient volumes. This will enable hospitals to proactively plan resource allocation and patient flow. The second com- ponent is a simulation model that evaluates the impact of different patient relocation strategies. The simulation will account for factors such as bed availability, staff capabilities, transportation logistics, and patient acuity to optimize the placement of patients across networked hospitals. Multiple scenarios will be tested, including inter-hospital trans- fers, use of temporary care facilities, and adaptations to discharge protocols. By combining predictive analytics and simulation modeling, this research aims to provide hospital administrators with a comprehensive decision-support tool. The proposed framework will empower them to anticipate demand, simulate relocation strategies, and imple- ment optimal policies to distribute patients and resources. Ultimately, this work seeks to enhance the resilience of healthcare systems in the face of COVID-19 and future pandemics.

zh.title: 优化大流行期间的医院容量：战略患者转诊的双组分框架
zh.summary: 本研究提出了一个两部分框架，结合时间序列预测和模拟建模，通过战略患者转诊来优化大流行期间的医院容量，帮助管理人员预测需求并高效分配资源。

fr.title: Optimiser la capacité hospitalière lors des pandémies : Un cadre à deux composantes pour la réinstallation stratégique des patients
fr.summary: Cette recherche propose un cadre à deux parties combinant la prédiction de séries chronologiques et la modélisation par simulation pour optimiser la capacité hospitalière lors des pandémies par la réinstallation stratégique des patients, aidant les administrateurs à anticiper la demande et à distribuer les ressources efficacement.

de.title: Optimierung der Krankenhauskapazität während Pandemien: Ein zweiteiliger Rahmen für strategische Patientenverlegung
de.summary: Diese Forschung schlägt einen zweiteiligen Rahmen vor, der Zeitreihenvorhersage und Simulationsmodellierung kombiniert, um die Krankenhauskapazität während Pandemien durch strategische Patientenverlegung zu optimieren und Administratoren dabei zu helfen, Nachfragen vorherzusagen und Ressourcen effizient zu verteilen.

es.title: Optimización de la Capacidad Hospitalaria Durante Pandemias: Un Marco Dual para la Reubicación Estratégica de Pacientes
es.summary: Esta investigación propone un marco de dos componentes que combina predicción de series temporales y modelado de simulación para optimizar la capacidad hospitalaria durante pandemias mediante la reubicación estratégica de pacientes, ayudando a los administradores a anticipar la demanda y distribuir recursos de manera eficiente.

title: MAC: Multi-Agent Constitution Learning
summary: Multi-Agent Constitutional Learning (MAC) proposes an improved method for automatically learning AI rules by using specialized agents to optimize structured rule sets, outperforming existing methods by over 50% while producing interpretable and auditable rules.
content:
arXiv:2603.15968v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Constitutional AI is a method to oversee and control LLMs based on a set of rules written in natural language. These rules are typically written by human experts, but could in principle be learned automatically given sufficient training data for the desired behavior. Existing LLM-based prompt optimizers attempt this but are ineffective at learning constitutions since (i) they require many labeled examples and (ii) lack structure in the optimized prompts, leading to diminishing improvements as prompt size grows. To address these limitations, we propose Multi-Agent Constitutional Learning (MAC), which optimizes over structured prompts represented as sets of rules using a network of agents with specialized tasks to accept, edit, or reject rule updates. We also present MAC+, which improves performance by training agents on successful trajectories to reinforce updates leading to higher reward. We evaluate MAC on tagging Personally Identifiable Information (PII), a classification task with limited labels where interpretability is critical, and demonstrate that it generalizes to other agentic tasks such as tool calling. MAC outperforms recent prompt optimization methods by over 50%, produces human-readable and auditable rule sets, and achieves performance comparable to supervised fine-tuning and GRPO without requiring parameter updates.

zh.title: MAC：多智能体宪法学习
zh.summary: 多智能体宪法学习（MAC）提出了一种改进的方法，通过使用专门的智能体来优化结构化规则集以自动学习人工智能规则，相比现有方法性能提高超过50%，同时产生可解释和可审计的规则。

fr.title: MAC : Apprentissage constitutionnel multi-agents
fr.summary: L'apprentissage constitutionnel multi-agents (MAC) propose une méthode améliorée pour apprendre automatiquement les règles d'IA en utilisant des agents spécialisés pour optimiser des ensembles de règles structurés, surpassant les méthodes existantes de plus de 50% tout en produisant des règles interprétables et auditables.

de.title: MAC: Multi-Agent-Verfassungslernen
de.summary: Das Multi-Agent Constitutional Learning (MAC) schlägt eine verbesserte Methode zum automatischen Lernen von KI-Regeln vor, indem spezialisierte Agenten verwendet werden, um strukturierte Regelsätze zu optimieren, die bestehende Methoden um über 50% übertreffen und gleichzeitig interpretierbare und prüfbare Regeln liefern.

es.title: MAC: Aprendizaje Constitucional Multi-Agente
es.summary: El Aprendizaje Constitucional Multi-Agente (MAC) propone un método mejorado para aprender automáticamente reglas de IA utilizando agentes especializados para optimizar conjuntos de reglas estructurados, superando los métodos existentes por más del 50% mientras produce reglas interpretables y auditables.

title: Safety is Non-Compositional: A Formal Framework for Capability-Based AI Systems
summary: This paper provides formal proof that safety properties are non-compositional in AI systems with conjunctive capability dependencies, meaning two individually safe agents can collectively reach forbidden goals—a critical finding for multi-agent safety design.
content:
arXiv:2603.15973v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper contains the first formal proof that safety is non-compositional in the presence of conjunctive capability dependencies: two agents each individually inca- pable of reaching any forbidden capability can, when combined, collectively reach a forbidden goal through an emergent conjunctive dependency.

zh.title: 安全性是非组合的：基于能力的AI系统正式框架
zh.summary: 本文提供了正式证明，说明在具有合取能力依赖关系的AI系统中，安全属性是非组合的，这意味着两个单独安全的代理可以共同达到禁止的目标——这是多代理安全设计的关键发现。

fr.title: La sécurité n'est pas compositionnelle : un cadre formel pour les systèmes d'IA basés sur les capacités
fr.summary: Cet article fournit une preuve formelle que les propriétés de sécurité ne sont pas compositionnelles dans les systèmes d'IA avec des dépendances de capacité conjonctives, ce qui signifie que deux agents individuellement sûrs peuvent collectivement atteindre des objectifs interdits, une conclusion critique pour la conception de la sécurité multi-agents.

de.title: Sicherheit ist nicht-kompositionell: Ein formales Rahmenwerk für fähigkeitsbasierte KI-Systeme
de.summary: Dieses Papier bietet einen formalen Beweis, dass Sicherheitseigenschaften in KI-Systemen mit konjunktiven Fähigkeitsabhängigkeiten nicht-kompositionell sind, was bedeutet, dass zwei einzeln sichere Agenten gemeinsam verbotene Ziele erreichen können – eine kritische Erkenntnis für das Design der Multi-Agent-Sicherheit.

es.title: La seguridad no es compositiva: un marco formal para sistemas de IA basados en capacidades
es.summary: Este artículo proporciona una prueba formal de que las propiedades de seguridad no son compositivas en sistemas de IA con dependencias de capacidad conjuntivas, lo que significa que dos agentes individualmente seguros pueden alcanzar colectivamente objetivos prohibidos, un hallazgo crítico para el diseño de seguridad multiagente.

title: An Agentic Evaluation Framework for AI-Generated Scientific Code in PETSc
summary: The petscagent-bench framework uses an agent-evaluating-agents paradigm for comprehensive evaluation of AI-generated scientific code across correctness, performance, and library-specific conventions, revealing that models often miss domain-specific requirements despite generating readable code.
content:
arXiv:2603.15976v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While large language models have significantly accelerated scientific code generation, comprehensively evaluating the generated code remains a major challenge. Traditional benchmarks reduce evaluation to test-case matching, an approach insufficient for library code in HPC where solver selection, API conventions, memory management, and performance are just as critical as functional correctness. To address this gap, we introduce petscagent-bench, an agentic framework built on an agents-evaluating-agents paradigm. Instead of relying on static scripts, petscagent-bench deploys a tool-augmented evaluator agent that compiles, executes, and measures code produced by a separate model-under-test agent, orchestrating a 14-evaluator pipeline across five scoring categories: correctness, performance, code quality, algorithmic appropriateness, and library-specific conventions. Because the agents communicate through standardized protocols (A2A and MCP), the framework enables black-box evaluation of any coding agent without requiring access to its source code. We demonstrate the framework on a benchmark suite of realistic problems using the PETSc library for HPC. Our empirical analysis of frontier models reveals that while current models generate readable, well-structured code, they consistently struggle with library-specific conventions that traditional pass/fail metrics completely miss.

zh.title: PETSc中AI生成科学代码的代理评估框架
zh.summary: petscagent-bench框架采用代理评估代理的范式，对AI生成的科学代码进行全面评估，涵盖正确性、性能和库特定的约定，揭示了模型尽管生成可读代码但通常会忽略特定领域的需求。

fr.title: Un cadre d'évaluation d'agent pour le code scientifique généré par l'IA dans PETSc
fr.summary: Le cadre petscagent-bench utilise un paradigme d'agent évaluant les agents pour une évaluation complète du code scientifique généré par l'IA en termes de correction, de performance et de conventions spécifiques à la bibliothèque, révélant que les modèles ratent souvent les exigences propres au domaine malgré la génération de code lisible.

de.title: Ein Agent-Evaluierungs-Framework für KI-generierte wissenschaftliche Code in PETSc
de.summary: Das petscagent-bench-Framework verwendet ein Agent-evaluiert-Agenten-Paradigma für eine umfassende Bewertung von KI-generiertem wissenschaftlichem Code in Bezug auf Korrektheit, Leistung und bibliotheksspezifische Konventionen, und zeigt, dass Modelle häufig domänenspezifische Anforderungen übersehen, obwohl sie lesbaren Code generieren.

es.title: Un marco de evaluación de agentes para código científico generado por IA en PETSc
es.summary: El marco petscagent-bench utiliza un paradigma de agente evaluando agentes para una evaluación integral del código científico generado por IA en términos de corrección, rendimiento y convenciones específicas de la biblioteca, revelando que los modelos a menudo pierden requisitos específicos del dominio a pesar de generar código legible.

title: From Workflow Automation to Capability Closure: A Formal Framework for Safe and Revenue-Aware Customer Service AI
summary: This paper addresses a critical safety gap where individually safe customer service agents can combine through emergent dependencies to reach forbidden goals, proposing a formal framework for safe and revenue-aware automation in multi-agent networks.
content:
arXiv:2603.15978v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Customer service automation is undergoing a structural transformation. The dominant paradigm is shifting from scripted chatbots and single-agent responders toward networks of specialised AI agents that compose capabilities dynamically across billing, service provision, payments, and fulfilment. This shift introduces a safety gap that no current platform has closed: two agents individually verified as safe can, when combined, reach a forbidden goal through an emergent conjunctive dependency that neither possesses alone.

zh.title: 从工作流自动化到能力闭包：安全和收益意识的客户服务AI正式框架
zh.summary: 本文解决了一个关键的安全漏洞，其中单独安全的客户服务代理可以通过出现的依赖关系结合以达到禁止的目标，提出了一个针对多代理网络中安全和收益意识自动化的正式框架。

fr.title: De l'automatisation des flux de travail à la fermeture des capacités : un cadre formel pour l'IA de service client sûr et sensible au revenu
fr.summary: Cet article aborde une lacune de sécurité critique où les agents de service client individuellement sûrs peuvent se combiner par le biais de dépendances émergentes pour atteindre des objectifs interdits, en proposant un cadre formel pour l'automatisation sûre et consciente des revenus dans les réseaux multi-agents.

de.title: Von der Workflow-Automatisierung zur Capability Closure: Ein formales Rahmenwerk für sichere und umsatzorientierte KI im Kundendienst
de.summary: Dieses Papier behandelt eine kritische Sicherheitslücke, bei der einzeln sichere Kundenservice-Agenten sich durch entstehende Abhängigkeiten verbinden können, um verbotene Ziele zu erreichen, und schlägt ein formales Rahmenwerk für sichere und umsatzorientierte Automatisierung in Multi-Agent-Netzwerken vor.

es.title: De la automatización de flujos de trabajo al cierre de capacidades: un marco formal para la IA de servicio al cliente segura y consciente del ingresos
es.summary: Este artículo aborda una brecha de seguridad crítica donde agentes de servicio al cliente individualmente seguros pueden combinarse a través de dependencias emergentes para alcanzar objetivos prohibidos, proponiendo un marco formal para la automatización segura y consciente de ingresos en redes multiagente.

title: Selective Memory for Artificial Intelligence: Write-Time Gating with Hierarchical Archiving
summary: Write-time gating filters incoming knowledge using composite salience scores to improve retrieval-augmented generation, achieving 100% accuracy versus 13% for ungated systems and significantly outperforming read-time filtering approaches at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.15994v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation stores all content indiscriminately, degrading accuracy as noise accumulates. Parametric approaches compress knowledge into weights, precluding selective updates. Neither mirrors biological memory, which gates encoding based on salience and archives rather than deletes superseded information. We introduce write-time gating that filters incoming knowledge objects using composite salience scores (source reputation, novelty, reliability) while maintaining version chains that preserve prior states. Using real LLM evaluation without oracle access to quality labels, write gating achieves 100 percent accuracy versus 13 percent for ungated stores. The critical finding emerges under distractor scaling: at 8:1 distractor ratios, read-time filtering (Self-RAG) collapses to 0 percent while write gating maintains 100 percent, revealing a structural advantage of write-time over read-time curation. Validation on Wikipedia (20 entities), procedurally generated pharmacology data, and 2026 arXiv papers confirms these findings. The gating advantage scales inversely with parametric memory support: +25pp for Wikipedia, +48pp for post-cutoff arXiv, +65pp for procedural data with zero training knowledge. Signal ablation confirms the method does not depend on oracle-correlated metadata. Write gating matches Self-RAG accuracy at one-ninth the query-time cost.

zh.title: 人工智能的选择性记忆：具有层次归档的写入时间门控
zh.summary: 写入时间门控使用复合显著性分数过滤传入知识以改进检索增强生成，相比未门控系统的13%实现了100%的准确性，并在规模上明显优于读取时间过滤方法。

fr.title: Mémoire sélective pour l'intelligence artificielle : filtrage en temps d'écriture avec archivage hiérarchique
fr.summary: Le filtrage en temps d'écriture filtre les connaissances entrantes en utilisant des scores de saillance composite pour améliorer la génération augmentée par récupération, atteignant une précision de 100% par rapport à 13% pour les systèmes non filtrés et surpassant considérablement les approches de filtrage en temps de lecture à grande échelle.

de.title: Selektives Gedächtnis für künstliche Intelligenz: Schreibzeit-Gating mit hierarchischer Archivierung
de.summary: Schreibzeit-Gating filtert eingehende Erkenntnisse mithilfe zusammengesetzter Prominenzscores, um die abrufbasierte Generierung zu verbessern und eine Genauigkeit von 100% gegenüber 13% für nicht gefilterte Systeme zu erreichen und Lesezeitfilterungsansätze bei großem Umfang erheblich zu übertreffen.

es.title: Memoria Selectiva para Inteligencia Artificial: Puerta en Tiempo de Escritura con Archivado Jerárquico
es.summary: El control de escritura filtra el conocimiento entrante utilizando puntuaciones de prominencia compuesta para mejorar la generación aumentada por recuperación, logrando una precisión del 100% frente al 13% para sistemas sin control y superando significativamente los enfoques de filtrado en tiempo de lectura a escala.

title: IRAM-Omega-Q: A Computational Architecture for Uncertainty Regulation in Artificial Agents
summary: IRAM-Omega-Q presents a computational architecture using quantum-like state representations to model internal regulation and uncertainty management in artificial agents, identifying reproducible critical boundaries for stability under stochastic perturbation.
content:
arXiv:2603.16020v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Artificial agents can achieve strong task performance while remaining opaque with respect to internal regulation, uncertainty management, and stability under stochastic perturbation. We present IRAM-Omega-Q, a computational architecture that models internal regulation as closed-loop control over a quantum-like state representation. The framework uses density matrices instrumentally as abstract state descriptors, enabling direct computation of entropy, purity, and coherence-related metrics without invoking physical quantum processes. A central adaptive gain is updated continuously to maintain a target uncertainty regime under noise. Using systematic parameter sweeps, fixed-seed publication-mode simulations, and susceptibility-based phase-diagram analysis, we identify reproducible critical boundaries in regulation-noise space. We further show that alternative control update orderings, interpreted as perception-first and action-first architectures, induce distinct stability regimes under identical external conditions. These results support uncertainty regulation as a concrete architectural principle for artificial agents and provide a formal setting for studying stability, control, and order effects in cognitively inspired AI systems. The framework is presented as a technical model of adaptive regulation dynamics in artificial agents. It makes no claims regarding phenomenological consciousness, and the quantum-like formalism is used strictly as a mathematical representation for structured uncertainty and state evolution.

zh.title: IRAM-Omega-Q：人工智能体不确定性调节的计算架构
zh.summary: IRAM-Omega-Q提出了一种计算架构，使用量子态表示来模拟人工智能体中的内部调节和不确定性管理，确定了在随机扰动下的可重现的关键边界以实现稳定性。

fr.title: IRAM-Omega-Q : Une architecture de calcul pour la régulation de l'incertitude dans les agents artificiels
fr.summary: IRAM-Omega-Q présente une architecture de calcul utilisant des représentations d'état de type quantique pour modéliser la régulation interne et la gestion de l'incertitude chez les agents artificiels, identifiant des limites critiques reproductibles pour la stabilité sous perturbation stochastique.

de.title: IRAM-Omega-Q: Eine Computerarchitektur zur Unsicherheitsregulierung in künstlichen Agenten
de.summary: IRAM-Omega-Q stellt eine Computerarchitektur vor, die quantenähnliche Zustandsdarstellungen verwendet, um die interne Regulierung und Unsicherheitsverwaltung in künstlichen Agenten zu modellieren und identifiziert reproduzierbare kritische Grenzen für Stabilität unter stochastischen Störungen.

es.title: IRAM-Omega-Q: Una Arquitectura Computacional para la Regulación de Incertidumbre en Agentes Artificiales
es.summary: IRAM-Omega-Q presenta una arquitectura computacional que utiliza representaciones de estado similares a cuántica para modelar la regulación interna y la gestión de la incertidumbre en agentes artificiales, identificando límites críticos reproducibles para la estabilidad bajo perturbación estocástica.

title: Interpretable Context Methodology: Folder Structure as Agentic Architecture
summary: Model Workspace Protocol (MWP) replaces complex multi-agent frameworks with a simpler filesystem-based approach using numbered folders and markdown files for sequential AI workflows, reducing engineering overhead while maintaining clarity and auditability.
content:
arXiv:2603.16021v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Current approaches to AI agent orchestration typically involve building multi-agent frameworks that manage context passing, memory, error handling, and step coordination through code. These frameworks work well for complex, concurrent systems. But for sequential workflows where a human reviews output at each step, they introduce engineering overhead that the problem does not require. This paper presents Model Workspace Protocol (MWP), a method that replaces framework-level orchestration with filesystem structure. Numbered folders represent stages. Plain markdown files carry the prompts and context that tell a single AI agent what role to play at each step. Local scripts handle the mechanical work that does not need AI at all. The result is a system where one agent, reading the right files at the right moment, does the work that would otherwise require a multi-agent framework. This approach applies ideas from Unix pipeline design, modular decomposition, multi-pass compilation, and literate programming to the specific problem of structuring context for AI agents. The protocol is open source under the MIT license.

zh.title: 可解释的上下文方法论：文件夹结构作为智能体架构
zh.summary: 模型工作区协议（MWP）用更简单的基于文件系统的方法取代复杂的多智能体框架，使用编号文件夹和标记文件进行顺序AI工作流，在保持清晰性和可审计性的同时减少了工程开销。

fr.title: Méthodologie de contexte interprétable : Structure des dossiers en tant qu'architecture d'agent
fr.summary: Le protocole Model Workspace (MWP) remplace les cadres multi-agents complexes par une approche plus simple basée sur le système de fichiers utilisant des dossiers numérotés et des fichiers markdown pour les flux de travail IA séquentiels, réduisant la surcharge d'ingénierie tout en maintenant la clarté et l'auditabilité.

de.title: Interpretierbare Kontextmethodologie: Ordnerstruktur als Agenten-Architektur
de.summary: Model Workspace Protocol (MWP) ersetzt komplexe Multi-Agent-Frameworks durch einen einfacheren dateisystemgestützten Ansatz mit nummerierten Ordnern und Markdown-Dateien für sequenzielle KI-Workflows und reduziert damit den Engineeringaufwand, während Klarheit und Prüfbarkeit erhalten bleiben.

es.title: Metodología de Contexto Interpretable: Estructura de Carpetas como Arquitectura de Agente
es.summary: El Protocolo Model Workspace (MWP) reemplaza marcos complejos de múltiples agentes con un enfoque más simple basado en el sistema de archivos que utiliza carpetas numeradas y archivos markdown para flujos de trabajo de IA secuenciales, reduciendo la sobrecarga de ingeniería mientras se mantiene la claridad y la auditabilidad.

title: Enhancing Linguistic Generalization of VLA: Fine-Tuning OpenVLA via Synthetic Instruction Augmentation
summary: This paper proposes parameter-efficient fine-tuning for OpenVLA using synthetic instruction augmentation to improve robotic task generalization, leveraging LLM-generated diverse commands and LoRA to help robots better understand natural language in novel environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.16044v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generalization remains a core challenge in embodied AI, as robots must adapt to diverse environments. While OpenVLA represents the State-of-the-Art (SOTA) in Vision-Language-Action models by leveraging large-scale pre-training, its zero-shot performance can be limited when encountering completely new environments. This paper proposes a parameter-efficient fine-tuning strategy to enhance the linguistic generalization of OpenVLA by synthesizing a general instruction set for the Bridge Dataset V2. The paper leverages a Large Language Model (LLM) to generate a rich variety of semantically equivalent but structurally diverse commands for existing trajectories. In this experiment, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is implemented to fine-tune OpenVLA on augmented pairs, allowing the model to bridge the gap between complex natural language intent and robotic actions. Results demonstrate that the LoRA-enhanced model's robustness, suggesting that enriching the linguistic space of specialized datasets is crucial for embodied agents.

zh.title: 增强VLA的语言泛化：通过合成指令增强进行OpenVLA微调
zh.summary: 本文提出了一种参数高效的OpenVLA微调方法，使用合成指令增强来改进机器人任务泛化，利用LLM生成的多样化命令和LoRA帮助机器人在新环境中更好地理解自然语言。

fr.title: Amélioration de la généralisation linguistique de VLA : Fine-tuning d'OpenVLA via augmentation d'instructions synthétiques
fr.summary: Cet article propose un fine-tuning efficace en paramètres pour OpenVLA en utilisant l'augmentation d'instructions synthétiques pour améliorer la généralisation des tâches robotiques, en exploitant des commandes diversifiées générées par LLM et LoRA pour aider les robots à mieux comprendre le langage naturel dans de nouveaux environnements.

de.title: Verbesserung der linguistischen Generalisierung von VLA: Fine-Tuning von OpenVLA durch synthetische Anweisungsvergrößerung
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt ein parametereffizientes Fine-Tuning für OpenVLA vor, wobei synthetische Anweisungsvergrößerung verwendet wird, um die Verallgemeinerung von Roboteraufgaben zu verbessern, wobei LLM-generierte diverse Befehle und LoRA genutzt werden, um Robotern zu helfen, natürliche Sprache in neuen Umgebungen besser zu verstehen.

es.title: Mejora de la generalización lingüística de VLA: Fine-tuning de OpenVLA mediante aumento de instrucciones sintéticas
es.summary: Este artículo propone un fine-tuning eficiente en parámetros para OpenVLA utilizando aumento de instrucciones sintéticas para mejorar la generalización de tareas robóticas, aprovechando comandos diversos generados por LLM y LoRA para ayudar a los robots a comprender mejor el lenguaje natural en entornos novedosos.

title: POaaS: Minimal-Edit Prompt Optimization as a Service to Lift Accuracy and Cut Hallucinations on On-Device sLLMs
summary: POaaS introduces lightweight prompt optimization for on-device small language models, routing queries to specialized modules while maintaining strict length and drift constraints, improving both accuracy and factuality more efficiently than search-heavy methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.16045v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Small language models (sLLMs) are increasingly deployed on-device, where imperfect user prompts--typos, unclear intent, or missing context--can trigger factual errors and hallucinations. Existing automatic prompt optimization (APO) methods were designed for large cloud LLMs and rely on search that often produces long, structured instructions; when executed under an on-device constraint where the same small model must act as optimizer and solver, these pipelines can waste context and even hurt accuracy. We propose POaaS, a minimal-edit prompt optimization layer that routes each query to lightweight specialists (Cleaner, Paraphraser, Fact-Adder) and merges their outputs under strict drift and length constraints, with a conservative skip policy for well-formed prompts. Under a strict fixed-model setting with Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, POaaS improves both task accuracy and factuality while representative APO baselines degrade them, and POaaS recovers up to +7.4% under token deletion and mixup. Overall, per-query conservative optimization is a practical alternative to search-heavy APO for on-device sLLMs.

zh.title: POaaS：最小编辑提示优化服务，用于提高片上小语言模型的准确性和减少幻觉
zh.summary: POaaS为片上小语言模型引入了轻量级提示优化，将查询路由到专用模块，同时保持严格的长度和漂移约束，比搜索密集型方法更有效地改进准确性和事实性。

fr.title: POaaS : Optimisation minimale de prompt en tant que service pour améliorer la précision et réduire les hallucinations sur les sLLM sur appareil
fr.summary: POaaS introduit une optimisation légère des prompts pour les petits modèles de langage sur appareil, en acheminant les requêtes vers des modules spécialisés tout en maintenant des contraintes strictes de longueur et de dérive, améliorant à la fois la précision et la factualité plus efficacement que les méthodes basées sur la recherche.

de.title: POaaS: Minimale Prompt-Optimierung als Service zur Verbesserung der Genauigkeit und Reduktion von Halluzinationen auf On-Device sLLMs
de.summary: POaaS führt eine leichte Prompt-Optimierung für kleine Sprachmodelle auf dem Gerät ein und leitet Abfragen an spezialisierte Module weiter, während strikte Längen- und Driftbeschränkungen eingehalten werden, wobei sowohl Genauigkeit als auch Faktizität effizienter als suchintensive Methoden verbessert werden.

es.title: POaaS: Optimización mínima de prompt como servicio para mejorar la precisión y reducir alucinaciones en sLLMs en dispositivo
es.summary: POaaS introduce optimización de prompts ligera para modelos de lenguaje pequeños en dispositivo, enrutando consultas a módulos especializados mientras mantiene restricciones estrictas de longitud y desviación, mejorando tanto la precisión como la factualidad de manera más eficiente que los métodos basados en búsqueda.

title: A Context Alignment Pre-processor for Enhancing the Coherence of Human-LLM Dialog
summary: Context Alignment Pre-processor (C.A.P.) addresses contextual misalignment in long-term dialogue by expanding user input, retrieving weighted historical context, and initiating clarification protocols, shifting interactions from one-way commands to collaborative dialogue.
content:
arXiv:2603.16052v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have made remarkable progress in generating fluent text, but they still face a critical challenge of contextual misalignment in long-term and dynamic dialogue. When human users omit premises, simplify references, or shift context abruptly during interactions with LLMs, the models may fail to capture their actual intentions, producing mechanical or off-topic responses that weaken the collaborative potential of dialogue. To address this problem, this paper proposes a computational framework called the Context Alignment Pre-processor (C.A.P.). Rather than operating during generation, C.A.P. functions as a pre-processing module between user input and response generation. The framework includes three core processes: (1) semantic expansion, which extends a user instruction to a broader semantic span including its premises, literal meaning, and implications; (2) time-weighted context retrieval, which prioritizes recent dialogue history through a temporal decay function approximating human conversational focus; and (3) alignment verification and decision branching, which evaluates whether the dialogue remains on track by measuring the semantic similarity between the current prompt and the weighted historical context. When a significant deviation is detected, C.A.P. initiates a structured clarification protocol to help users and the system recalibrate the conversation. This study presents the architecture and theoretical basis of C.A.P., drawing on cognitive science and Common Ground theory in human-computer interaction. We argue that C.A.P. is not only a technical refinement but also a step toward shifting human-computer dialogue from one-way command-execution patterns to two-way, self-correcting, partnership-based collaboration. Finally, we discuss implementation paths, evaluation methods, and implications for the future design of interactive intelligent systems.

zh.title: 用于增强人类-LLM对话连贯性的上下文对齐预处理器
zh.summary: 上下文对齐预处理器（C.A.P.）通过扩展用户输入、检索加权历史上下文和启动澄清协议来解决长期对话中的上下文错位问题，将交互从单向命令转变为协作对话。

fr.title: Un préprocesseur d'alignement contextuel pour améliorer la cohérence du dialogue humain-LLM
fr.summary: Le préprocesseur d'alignement contextuel (C.A.P.) résout le désalignement contextuel dans le dialogue à long terme en développant l'entrée de l'utilisateur, en récupérant le contexte historique pondéré et en initiant des protocoles de clarification, transformant les interactions de commandes unidirectionnelles à dialogue collaboratif.

de.title: Ein Kontextausrichtungs-Vorprozessor zur Verbesserung der Kohärenz des Human-LLM-Dialogs
de.summary: Der Kontextausrichtungs-Vorprozessor (C.A.P.) behandelt kontextuelle Fehlausrichtung in langfristiger Kommunikation durch Erweiterung der Benutzereingabe, Abruf gewichteter historischer Kontexte und Einleitung von Klärungsprotokollen, wobei Interaktionen von einseitigen Befehlen zu kooperativem Dialog verschoben werden.

es.title: Un preprocesador de alineación contextual para mejorar la coherencia del diálogo humano-LLM
es.summary: El preprocesador de alineación contextual (C.A.P.) aborda la alineación contextual inadecuada en diálogos a largo plazo expandiendo la entrada del usuario, recuperando contexto histórico ponderado e iniciando protocolos de aclaración, cambiando las interacciones de comandos unidireccionales a diálogos colaborativos.

title: ARISE: Agent Reasoning with Intrinsic Skill Evolution in Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
summary: ARISE introduces a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework where a shared policy manages both high-level skills and low-level responses to improve mathematical reasoning, with a skill library that evolves during training to consistently outperform existing methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.16060v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The dominant paradigm for improving mathematical reasoning in language models relies on Reinforcement Learning with verifiable rewards. Yet existing methods treat each problem instance in isolation without leveraging the reusable strategies that emerge and accumulate during training. To this end, we introduce ARISE (Agent Reasoning via Intrinsic Skill Evolution), a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework, in which a shared policy operates both to manage skills at high-level and to generate responses at low-level (denoted as a Skills Manager and a Worker, respectively). The Manager maintains a tiered skill library through a dedicated skill generation rollout that performs structured summarization of successful solution traces (after execution), while employing a policy-driven selection mechanism to retrieve relevant skills to condition future rollouts (before execution). A hierarchical reward design guides the co-evolution of reasoning ability and library quality. Experiments on two base models and seven benchmarks spanning both competition mathematics and Omni-MATH show that ARISE consistently outperforms GRPO-family algorithms and memory-augmented baselines, with particularly notable gains on out-of-distribution tasks. Ablation studies confirm that each component contributes to the observed improvements and that library quality and reasoning performance improve in tandem throughout training. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Skylanding/ARISE}{https://github.com/Skylanding/ARISE}.

zh.title: ARISE：分层强化学习中具有内在技能演化的代理推理
zh.summary: ARISE引入了一个分层强化学习框架，其中共享策略管理高级技能和低级响应以改进数学推理，技能库在训练期间不断演化，能够始终超越现有方法。

fr.title: ARISE : Raisonnement d'agent avec évolution des compétences intrinsèques dans l'apprentissage par renforcement hiérarchique
fr.summary: ARISE introduit un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement hiérarchique où une politique partagée gère à la fois les compétences de haut niveau et les réponses de bas niveau pour améliorer le raisonnement mathématique, avec une bibliothèque de compétences qui évolue pendant l'entraînement pour surpasser constamment les méthodes existantes.

de.title: ARISE: Agenten-Reasoning mit intrinsischer Skill-Evolution im hierarchischen Reinforcement Learning
de.summary: ARISE führt ein hierarchisches Reinforcement-Learning-Framework ein, in dem eine gemeinsame Policy sowohl Hochebenen-Skills als auch Niedrigebenen-Reaktionen verwaltet, um mathematisches Reasoning zu verbessern, mit einer Skill-Bibliothek, die sich während des Trainings entwickelt und konsistent bestehende Methoden übertrifft.

es.title: ARISE: Razonamiento de Agente con Evolución de Habilidades Intrínsecas en Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Jerárquico
es.summary: ARISE introduce un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo jerárquico donde una política compartida gestiona tanto habilidades de alto nivel como respuestas de bajo nivel para mejorar el razonamiento matemático, con una biblioteca de habilidades que evoluciona durante el entrenamiento para superar consistentemente los métodos existentes.

title: VIGIL: Towards Edge-Extended Agentic AI for Enterprise IT Support
summary: VIGIL deploys desktop-resident agents for enterprise IT support that perform diagnosis and policy-governed remediation directly on user devices, reducing interaction rounds by 39%, achieving 4x faster diagnosis, and enabling self-service resolution in 82% of cases.
content:
arXiv:2603.16110v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Enterprise IT support is constrained by heterogeneous devices, evolving policies, and long-tail failure modes that are difficult to resolve centrally. We present VIGIL, an edge-extended agentic AI system that deploys desktop-resident agents to perform situated diagnosis, retrieval over enterprise knowledge, and policy-governed remediation directly on user devices with explicit consent and end-to-end observability. In a 10-week pilot of VIGIL's operational loop on 100 resource-constrained endpoints, VIGIL reduces interaction rounds by 39%, achieves at least 4 times faster diagnosis, and supports self-service resolution in 82% of matched cases. Users report excellent usability, high trust, and low cognitive workload across four validated instruments, with qualitative feedback highlighting transparency as critical for trust. Notably, users rated the system higher when no historical matches were available, suggesting on-device diagnosis provides value independent of knowledge base coverage. This pilot establishes safety and observability foundations for fleet-wide continuous improvement.

zh.title: VIGIL：面向企业IT支持的边缘扩展智能代理
zh.summary: VIGIL为企业IT支持部署桌面驻留代理，在用户设备上直接执行诊断和策略管控的修复，减少交互轮次39%，实现4倍更快的诊断，并在82%的案例中实现自助解决。

fr.title: VIGIL : Vers une IA Agentic étendue aux bords pour le support informatique d'entreprise
fr.summary: VIGIL déploie des agents résidents de bureau pour le support informatique d'entreprise qui effectuent des diagnostics et des remèdes gouvernés par des politiques directement sur les appareils des utilisateurs, réduisant les tours d'interaction de 39%, réalisant un diagnostic 4 fois plus rapide et permettant une résolution en libre-service dans 82% des cas.

de.title: VIGIL: Richtung Edge-erweiterte agentenbasierte KI für Unternehmens-IT-Support
de.summary: VIGIL setzt Desktop-resident Agenten für Enterprise-IT-Support ein, die Diagnose und richtliniengesteuerte Wiederherstellung direkt auf Benutzergeräten durchführen, wodurch Interaktionsrunden um 39% reduziert werden, eine 4x schnellere Diagnose erreicht wird und in 82% der Fälle eine Selbstbedienungslösung ermöglicht wird.

es.title: VIGIL: Hacia una IA Agentic extendida en los bordes para soporte de TI empresarial
es.summary: VIGIL despliega agentes residentes de escritorio para soporte de TI empresarial que realizan diagnóstico y remediación gobernada por políticas directamente en dispositivos de usuarios, reduciendo rondas de interacción en un 39%, logrando diagnóstico 4 veces más rápido y permitiendo resolución de autoservicio en el 82% de los casos.

title: NeuronSpark: A Spiking Neural Network Language Model with Selective State Space Dynamics
summary: NeuronSpark demonstrates that pure spiking neural networks can effectively learn large-scale language modeling from scratch without Transformer distillation, with a 0.9B-parameter model achieving competitive pretraining loss and early multi-turn dialogue behavior.
content:
arXiv:2603.16148v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We ask whether a pure spiking backbone can learn large-scale language modeling from random initialization, without Transformer distillation. We introduce NeuronSpark, a 0.9B-parameter SNN language model trained with next-token prediction and surrogate gradients. The model combines selective state-space spiking dynamics, leakage-current inter-layer communication, PonderNet adaptive timesteps, fused Triton PLIF kernels, and stabilization techniques (residual centering, lateral-inhibition normalization, and natural-gradient compensation). Under a constrained budget (about 1.4B pretraining tokens and 6.5K SFT steps), NeuronSpark-0.9B reaches 3.6 pretraining loss and shows early multi-turn dialogue behavior after SFT. These results support the feasibility of end-to-end language modeling with a pure SNN architecture at this scale.

zh.title: NeuronSpark：具有选择性状态空间动力学的脉冲神经网络语言模型
zh.summary: NeuronSpark证明了纯脉冲神经网络可以有效地从零开始学习大规模语言建模，无需Transformer蒸馏，其0.9B参数模型实现了具竞争力的预训练损失和早期多轮对话行为。

fr.title: NeuronSpark : Un modèle de langage de réseau neuronal pulsé avec dynamique sélective de l'espace d'état
fr.summary: NeuronSpark démontre que les réseaux de neurones pulsés purs peuvent apprendre efficacement la modélisation du langage à grande échelle à partir de zéro sans distillation Transformer, avec un modèle de 0,9B paramètres réalisant une perte de préentraînement compétitive et un comportement de dialogue multi-tour précoce.

de.title: NeuronSpark: Ein Spiking Neural Network Sprachmodell mit selektiver Zustandsraum-Dynamik
de.summary: NeuronSpark zeigt, dass reine Spiking Neural Networks effektiv großskalige Sprachmodellierung von Grund auf ohne Transformer-Destillation erlernen können, wobei ein 0,9B-Parameter-Modell wettbewerbsfähigen Vortrainings-Verlust und frühes Multi-Turn-Dialog-Verhalten erreicht.

es.title: NeuronSpark: Un modelo de lenguaje de red neuronal de picos con dinámicas de espacio de estado selectivas
es.summary: NeuronSpark demuestra que las redes neuronales de picos puros pueden aprender eficientemente el modelado del lenguaje a gran escala desde cero sin destilación de Transformers, con un modelo de 0,9B parámetros logrando pérdida de preentrenamiento competitiva y comportamiento de diálogo multi-turno temprano.

title: SQL-ASTRA: Alleviating Sparse Feedback in Agentic SQL via Column-Set Matching and Trajectory Aggregation
summary: This paper addresses the credit assignment problem in multi-turn Text-to-SQL tasks by proposing a two-tiered reward mechanism combining Aggregated Trajectory Reward (ATR) and Column-Set Matching Reward (CSMR). The approach achieves a 5% improvement over binary-reward baselines and outperforms state-of-the-art models on standard benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.16161v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) shows promise for complex tasks, but Text-to-SQL remains mostly restricted to single-turn paradigms. A primary bottleneck is the credit assignment problem. In traditional paradigms, rewards are determined solely by the final-turn feedback, which ignores the intermediate process and leads to ambiguous credit evaluation. To address this, we propose Agentic SQL, a framework featuring a universal two-tiered reward mechanism designed to provide effective trajectory-level evaluation and dense step-level signals. First, we introduce Aggregated Trajectory Reward (ATR) to resolve multi-turn credit assignment. Using an asymmetric transition matrix, ATR aggregates process-oriented scores to incentivize continuous improvement. Leveraging Lyapunov stability theory, we prove ATR acts as an energy dissipation operator, guaranteeing a cycle-free policy and monotonic convergence. Second, Column-Set Matching Reward (CSMR) provides immediate step-level rewards to mitigate sparsity. By executing queries at each turn, CSMR converts binary (0/1) feedback into dense [0, 1] signals based on partial correctness. Evaluations on BIRD show a 5% gain over binary-reward GRPO. Notably, our approach outperforms SOTA Arctic-Text2SQL-R1-7B on BIRD and Spider 2.0 using identical models, propelling Text-to-SQL toward a robust multi-turn agent paradigm.

zh.title: SQL-ASTRA：通过列集匹配和轨迹聚合缓解代理SQL中的稀疏反馈
zh.summary: 该论文通过提出结合聚合轨迹奖励（ATR）和列集匹配奖励（CSMR）的两层奖励机制，解决了多轮文本到SQL任务中的信用分配问题。该方法比二元奖励基线提高了5%，并在标准基准上优于最先进的模型。

fr.title: SQL-ASTRA: Atténuer les retours clairsemés dans SQL agentique via la correspondance d'ensembles de colonnes et l'agrégation de trajectoires
fr.summary: Cet article résout le problème d'attribution de crédit dans les tâches multi-tours de texte-vers-SQL en proposant un mécanisme de récompense à deux niveaux combinant la récompense de trajectoire agrégée (ATR) et la récompense de correspondance d'ensembles de colonnes (CSMR). L'approche atteint une amélioration de 5% par rapport aux bases de récompenses binaires et surpasse les modèles de pointe sur les benchmarks standard.

de.title: SQL-ASTRA: Abschwächung von spärlichem Feedback in agentengestütztem SQL durch Spaltenmenge-Matching und Trajektorienaggregation
de.summary: Dieses Papier behandelt das Kreditvergabeproblem in mehrstufigen Text-zu-SQL-Aufgaben, indem ein zweistufiger Belohnungsmechanismus vorgeschlagen wird, der die aggregierte Trajektorien-Belohnung (ATR) und die Spaltenmenge-Matching-Belohnung (CSMR) kombiniert. Der Ansatz erreicht eine 5%ige Verbesserung gegenüber binären Belohnungsbaselines und übertrifft modernste Modelle auf Standard-Benchmarks.

es.title: SQL-ASTRA: Aliviando la retroalimentación dispersa en SQL agentivo mediante coincidencia de conjuntos de columnas y agregación de trayectorias
es.summary: Este artículo aborda el problema de asignación de crédito en tareas multi-turno de Texto a SQL proponiendo un mecanismo de recompensa de dos niveles que combina la Recompensa de Trayectoria Agregada (ATR) y la Recompensa de Coincidencia de Conjuntos de Columnas (CSMR). El enfoque logra una mejora del 5% sobre las líneas de base de recompensa binaria y supera a los modelos de última generación en puntos de referencia estándar.

title: Are Large Language Models Truly Smarter Than Humans?
summary: This study audits six frontier LLMs for data contamination on MMLU benchmarks, finding 13.8% overall contamination with rates up to 66.7% in specific subjects. The research demonstrates that apparent superior LLM performance may be substantially inflated by training data overlap with evaluation sets.
content:
arXiv:2603.16197v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Public leaderboards increasingly suggest that large language models (LLMs) surpass human experts on benchmarks spanning academic knowledge, law, and programming. Yet most benchmarks are fully public, their questions widely mirrored across the internet, creating systematic risk that models were trained on the very data used to evaluate them. This paper presents three complementary experiments forming a rigorous multi-method contamination audit of six frontier LLMs: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, DeepSeek-R1, DeepSeek-V3, Llama-3.3-70B, and Qwen3-235B. Experiment 1 applies a lexical contamination detection pipeline to 513 MMLU questions across all 57 subjects, finding an overall contamination rate of 13.8% (18.1% in STEM, up to 66.7% in Philosophy) and estimated performance gains of +0.030 to +0.054 accuracy points by category. Experiment 2 applies a paraphrase and indirect-reference diagnostic to 100 MMLU questions, finding accuracy drops by an average of 7.0 percentage points under indirect reference, rising to 19.8 pp in both Law and Ethics. Experiment 3 applies TS-Guessing behavioral probes to all 513 questions and all six models, finding that 72.5% trigger memorization signals far above chance, with DeepSeek-R1 displaying a distributed memorization signature (76.6% partial reconstruction, 0% verbatim recall) that explains its anomalous Experiment 2 profile. All three experiments converge on the same contamination ranking: STEM > Professional > Social Sciences > Humanities.

zh.title: 大型语言模型真的比人类更聪明吗?
zh.summary: 该研究对六个前沿LLM进行了MMLU基准数据污染审计，发现总体污染率为13.8%，特定科目的污染率高达66.7%。该研究表明，表面上LLM的性能优势可能被训练数据与评估集的重叠大幅夸大了。

fr.title: Les grands modèles de langage sont-ils vraiment plus intelligents que les humains?
fr.summary: Cette étude vérifie six LLM frontière pour la contamination des données sur les benchmarks MMLU, trouvant une contamination globale de 13,8% avec des taux jusqu'à 66,7% dans des sujets spécifiques. La recherche démontre que la performance supérieure apparente des LLM peut être considérablement gonflée par le chevauchement des données d'entraînement avec les ensembles d'évaluation.

de.title: Sind große Sprachmodelle wirklich intelligenter als Menschen?
de.summary: Diese Studie überprüft sechs führende LLMs auf Datenkontamination in MMLU-Benchmarks und findet eine Gesamtkontamination von 13,8% mit Raten bis zu 66,7% in spezifischen Fächern. Die Forschung zeigt, dass die anscheinend überlegene LLM-Leistung durch die Überlappung von Trainingsdaten mit Evaluierungssätzen wesentlich aufgeblasen sein kann.

es.title: ¿Son los grandes modelos de lenguaje realmente más inteligentes que los humanos?
es.summary: Este estudio audita seis LLM de frontera para contaminación de datos en puntos de referencia MMLU, encontrando una contaminación general del 13,8% con tasas de hasta el 66,7% en temas específicos. La investigación demuestra que el rendimiento aparentemente superior del LLM puede estar sustancialmente inflado por la superposición de datos de entrenamiento con conjuntos de evaluación.

title: Proactive Rejection and Grounded Execution: A Dual-Stage Intent Analysis Paradigm for Safe and Efficient AIoT Smart Homes
summary: This paper proposes a dual-stage framework for safer LLM-based smart home control that separates high-level intent understanding from low-level execution verification. The framework achieves 58.56% exact match rate and 87.04% invalid instruction rejection while reducing unnecessary user queries from 57% to 29%.
content:
arXiv:2603.16207v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) transition from information providers to embodied agents in the Internet of Things (IoT), they face significant challenges regarding reliability and interaction efficiency. Direct execution of LLM-generated commands often leads to entity hallucinations (e.g., trying to control non-existent devices). Meanwhile, existing iterative frameworks (e.g., SAGE) suffer from the Interaction Frequency Dilemma, oscillating between reckless execution and excessive user questioning. To address these issues, we propose a Dual-Stage Intent-Aware (DS-IA) Framework. This framework separates high-level user intent understanding from low-level physical execution. Specifically, Stage 1 serves as a semantic firewall to filter out invalid instructions and resolve vague commands by checking the current state of the home. Stage 2 then employs a deterministic cascade verifier-a strict, step-by-step rule checker that verifies the room, device, and capability in sequence-to ensure the action is actually physically possible before execution. Extensive experiments on the HomeBench and SAGE benchmarks demonstrate that DS-IA achieves an Exact Match (EM) rate of 58.56% (outperforming baselines by over 28%) and improves the rejection rate of invalid instructions to 87.04%. Evaluations on the SAGE benchmark further reveal that DS-IA resolves the Interaction Frequency Dilemma by balancing proactive querying with state-based inference. Specifically, it boosts the Autonomous Success Rate (resolving tasks without unnecessary user intervention) from 42.86% to 71.43%, while maintaining high precision in identifying irreducible ambiguities that truly necessitate human clarification. These results underscore the framework's ability to minimize user disturbance through accurate environmental grounding.

zh.title: 主动拒绝和接地执行：针对安全高效AIoT智能家居的双阶段意图分析范式
zh.summary: 该论文为基于LLM的智能家居控制提出了一个双阶段框架，将高级意图理解与低级执行验证分离。该框架实现了58.56%的精确匹配率和87.04%的无效指令拒绝率，同时将不必要的用户查询从57%减少到29%。

fr.title: Rejet proactif et exécution ancrée: un paradigme d'analyse d'intention à double étape pour les maisons intelligentes AIoT sûres et efficaces
fr.summary: Cet article propose un cadre à deux étapes pour un contrôle plus sûr des maisons intelligentes basé sur les LLM qui sépare la compréhension d'intention de haut niveau de la vérification d'exécution de bas niveau. Le cadre atteint un taux de correspondance exacte de 58,56% et un taux de rejet d'instruction invalide de 87,04% tout en réduisant les requêtes utilisateur inutiles de 57% à 29%.

de.title: Proaktive Ablehnung und begründete Ausführung: Ein zweistufiges Intent-Analyseparadigma für sichere und effiziente AIoT-Smart-Homes
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt ein zweistufiges Framework für eine sicherere LLM-basierte Smart-Home-Kontrolle vor, das das Verständnis von Hochebenen-Intent von der Verifikation der Niedrigebenen-Ausführung trennt. Das Framework erreicht eine genaue Übereinstimmungsrate von 58,56% und eine ungültige Anweisungsablehnungsrate von 87,04%, während es unnötige Benutzerabfragen von 57% auf 29% reduziert.

es.title: Rechazo proactivo y ejecución fundamentada: un paradigma de análisis de intención de doble etapa para hogares inteligentes AIoT seguros y eficientes
es.summary: Este artículo propone un marco de dos etapas para un control más seguro de hogares inteligentes basado en LLM que separa la comprensión de intención de alto nivel de la verificación de ejecución de bajo nivel. El marco logra una tasa de coincidencia exacta del 58,56% y una tasa de rechazo de instrucciones inválidas del 87,04%, mientras reduce las consultas innecesarias del usuario del 57% al 29%.

title: MOSAIC: Composable Safety Alignment with Modular Control Tokens
summary: This work introduces MOSAIC, a modular framework for context-dependent safety alignment in LLMs using learnable control tokens that can be composed and activated at inference time. The approach enables customizable constraints for different users and applications while maintaining model utility.
content:
arXiv:2603.16210v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Safety alignment in large language models (LLMs) is commonly implemented as a single static policy embedded in model parameters. However, real-world deployments often require context-dependent safety rules that vary across users, regions, and applications. Existing approaches struggle to provide such conditional control: parameter-level alignment entangles safety behaviors with general capabilities, while prompt-based methods rely on natural language instructions that provide weak enforcement. We propose MOSAIC, a modular framework that enables compositional safety alignment through learnable control tokens optimized over a frozen backbone model. Each token represents a safety constraint and can be flexibly activated and composed at inference time. To train compositional tokens efficiently, we introduce order-based task sampling and a distribution-level alignment objective that mitigates over-refusal.
