Experiments show that MOSAIC achieves strong defense performance with substantially lower over-refusal while preserving model utility.

zh.title: MOSAIC：具有模块化控制令牌的可组合安全对齐
zh.summary: 这项工作介绍了MOSAIC，一个模块化框架，用于使用可学习的控制令牌在LLM中进行上下文相关的安全对齐，这些令牌可以在推理时进行组合和激活。该方法为不同的用户和应用程序启用可定制的约束，同时保持模型实用性。

fr.title: MOSAIC : Alignement de sécurité composable avec jetons de contrôle modulaires
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit MOSAIC, un cadre modulaire pour l'alignement de la sécurité dépendant du contexte dans les LLM utilisant des jetons de contrôle apprenables qui peuvent être composés et activés au moment de l'inférence. L'approche permet des contraintes personnalisables pour différents utilisateurs et applications tout en maintenant l'utilité du modèle.

de.title: MOSAIC: Zusammensetzbare Sicherheitsausrichtung mit modularen Kontrolltoken
de.summary: Diese Arbeit stellt MOSAIC vor, ein modulares Framework für kontextabhängige Sicherheitsausrichtung in LLMs unter Verwendung von lernbaren Kontrolltoken, die zum Inferenzzeitpunkt zusammengesetzt und aktiviert werden können. Der Ansatz ermöglicht anpassbare Beschränkungen für verschiedene Benutzer und Anwendungen und behält gleichzeitig den Modellnutzen bei.

es.title: MOSAIC: Alineación de seguridad composable con tokens de control modulares
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce MOSAIC, un marco modular para la alineación de seguridad dependiente del contexto en LLM utilizando tokens de control aprendibles que se pueden componer y activar en el momento de la inferencia. El enfoque permite restricciones personalizables para diferentes usuarios y aplicaciones mientras mantiene la utilidad del modelo.

title: Adaptive Theory of Mind for LLM-based Multi-Agent Coordination
summary: This paper demonstrates that misalignment in theory-of-mind reasoning depth between agents impairs multi-agent coordination and proposes an adaptive ToM approach that estimates partners' reasoning levels. Evaluations on coordination tasks confirm the framework improves behavioral alignment and task success rates.
content:
arXiv:2603.16264v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM) refers to the ability to reason about others' mental states, and higher-order ToM involves considering that others also possess their own ToM. Equipping large language model (LLM)-driven agents with ToM has long been considered to improve their coordination in multiagent collaborative tasks. However, we find that misaligned ToM orders-mismatches in the depth of ToM reasoning between agents-can lead to insufficient or excessive reasoning about others, thereby impairing their coordination. To address this issue, we design an adaptive ToM (A-ToM) agent, which can align in ToM orders with its partner. Based on prior interactions, the agent estimates the partner's likely ToM order and leverages this estimation to predict the partner's action, thereby facilitating behavioral coordination. We conduct empirical evaluations on four multi-agent coordination tasks: a repeated matrix game, two grid navigation tasks and an Overcooked task. The results validate our findings on ToM alignment and demonstrate the effectiveness of our A-ToM agent. Furthermore, we discuss the generalizability of our A-ToM to non-LLM-based agents, as well as what would diminish the importance of ToM alignment.

zh.title: 用于基于LLM的多智能体协调的自适应心智理论
zh.summary: 本文演示了智能体之间心智理论推理深度的错位会损害多智能体协调，并提出了一个自适应ToM方法来估计合作伙伴的推理水平。在协调任务上的评估确认了该框架提高了行为对齐和任务成功率。

fr.title: Théorie adaptative de l'esprit pour la coordination multi-agents basée sur les LLM
fr.summary: Cet article démontre que le désalignement dans la profondeur du raisonnement de la théorie de l'esprit entre les agents entrave la coordination multi-agents et propose une approche ToM adaptative qui estime les niveaux de raisonnement des partenaires. Les évaluations sur les tâches de coordination confirment que le cadre améliore l'alignement comportemental et les taux de succès des tâches.

de.title: Adaptive Theorie des Geistes für LLM-basierte Multi-Agent-Koordination
de.summary: Dieses Papier demonstriert, dass eine Fehlausrichtung in der Tiefe des Theorie-des-Geistes-Denkens zwischen Agenten die Multi-Agent-Koordination beeinträchtigt, und schlägt einen adaptiven ToM-Ansatz vor, der die Denkniveaus der Partner schätzt. Bewertungen bei Koordinationsaufgaben bestätigen, dass das Framework die Verhaltensausrichtung und Aufgabenerfolgsquoten verbessert.

es.title: Teoría adaptativa de la mente para la coordinación multiagente basada en LLM
es.summary: Este documento demuestra que el desalineamiento en la profundidad del razonamiento de la teoría de la mente entre agentes afecta la coordinación multiagente y propone un enfoque ToM adaptativo que estima los niveles de razonamiento de los socios. Las evaluaciones en tareas de coordinación confirman que el marco mejora la alineación del comportamiento y las tasas de éxito de las tareas.

title: NeSy-Route: A Neuro-Symbolic Benchmark for Constrained Route Planning in Remote Sensing
summary: NeSy-Route introduces a large-scale neuro-symbolic benchmark with 10,821 route-planning tasks for evaluating multimodal LLMs on constrained planning in remote sensing. Comprehensive evaluation reveals significant deficiencies in perception and planning capabilities despite strong performance on perception-only tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.16307v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Remote sensing underpins crucial applications such as disaster relief and ecological field surveys, where systems must understand complex scenes and constraints and make reliable decisions. Current remote-sensing benchmarks mainly focus on evaluating perception and reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). They fail to assess planning capability, stemming either from the difficulty of curating and validating planning tasks at scale or from evaluation protocols that are inaccurate and inadequate. To address these limitations, we introduce NeSy-Route, a large-scale neuro-symbolic benchmark for constrained route planning in remote sensing. Within this benchmark, we introduce an automated data-generation framework that integrates high-fidelity semantic masks with heuristic search to produce diverse route-planning tasks with provably optimal solutions. This allows NeSy-Route to comprehensively evaluate planning across 10,821 route-planning samples, nearly 10 times larger than the largest prior benchmark. Furthermore, a three-level hierarchical neuro-symbolic evaluation protocol is developed to enable accurate assessment and support fine-grained analysis on perception, reasoning, and planning simultaneously. Our comprehensive evaluation of various state-of-the-art MLLMs demonstrates that existing MLLMs show significant deficiencies in perception and planning capabilities. We hope NeSy-Route can support further research and development of more powerful MLLMs for remote sensing.

zh.title: NeSy-Route：遥感中受约束路线规划的神经符号基准
zh.summary: NeSy-Route引入了一个大规模的神经符号基准，包含10,821个路线规划任务，用于评估多模态LLM在遥感中的受约束规划。全面的评估揭示了感知和规划能力的显著缺陷，尽管在仅感知任务上的表现很强。

fr.title: NeSy-Route : Un benchmark neuro-symbolique pour la planification d'itinéraire contrainte en télédétection
fr.summary: NeSy-Route introduit un benchmark neuro-symbolique à grande échelle avec 10 821 tâches de planification d'itinéraire pour évaluer les LLM multimodaux sur la planification contrainte en télédétection. L'évaluation complète révèle des déficiences importantes dans les capacités de perception et de planification malgré de bonnes performances sur les tâches de perception uniquement.

de.title: NeSy-Route: Ein neuro-symbolisches Benchmark für eingeschränkte Routenplanung in der Fernerkundung
de.summary: NeSy-Route führt einen großmaßstäblichen neuro-symbolischen Benchmark mit 10.821 Routenplanungsaufgaben ein, um multimodale LLMs bei eingeschränkter Planung in der Fernerkundung zu bewerten. Eine umfassende Bewertung zeigt erhebliche Mängel bei Wahrnehmungs- und Planungsfähigkeiten trotz starker Leistung bei reinen Wahrnehmungsaufgaben.

es.title: NeSy-Route: Un benchmark neuro-simbólico para la planificación de rutas restringidas en detección remota
es.summary: NeSy-Route introduce un benchmark neuro-simbólico a gran escala con 10,821 tareas de planificación de rutas para evaluar LLM multimodales en la planificación restringida en detección remota. La evaluación integral revela deficiencias significativas en las capacidades de percepción y planificación a pesar del buen desempeño en tareas de solo percepción.

title: Learning to Predict, Discover, and Reason in High-Dimensional Discrete Event Sequences
summary: This thesis addresses automated fault diagnostics in vehicles by combining event sequence modeling, causal discovery, and LLMs to process high-dimensional diagnostic sequences with thousands of unique codes. The framework introduces Transformer architectures and multi-agent systems for automated synthesis of error pattern rules.
content:
arXiv:2603.16313v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Electronic control units (ECUs) embedded within modern vehicles generate a large number of asynchronous events known as diagnostic trouble codes (DTCs). These discrete events form complex temporal sequences that reflect the evolving health of the vehicle's subsystems. In the automotive industry, domain experts manually group these codes into higher-level error patterns (EPs) using Boolean rules to characterize system faults and ensure safety. However, as vehicle complexity grows, this manual process becomes increasingly costly, error-prone, and difficult to scale. Notably, the number of unique DTCs in a modern vehicle is on the same order of magnitude as the vocabulary of a natural language, often numbering in the tens of thousands. This observation motivates a paradigm shift: treating diagnostic sequences as a language that can be modeled, predicted, and ultimately explained. Traditional statistical approaches fail to capture the rich dependencies and do not scale to high-dimensional datasets characterized by thousands of nodes, large sample sizes, and long sequence lengths. Specifically, the high cardinality of categorical event spaces in industrial logs poses a significant challenge, necessitating new machine learning architectures tailored to such event-driven systems. This thesis addresses automated fault diagnostics by unifying event sequence modeling, causal discovery, and large language models (LLMs) into a coherent framework for high-dimensional event streams. It is structured in three parts, reflecting a progressive transition from prediction to causal understanding and finally to reasoning for vehicle diagnostics. Consequently, we introduce several Transformer-based architectures for predictive maintenance, scalable sample- and population-level causal discovery frameworks and a multi-agent system that automates the synthesis of Boolean EP rules.

zh.title: 学习在高维离散事件序列中预测、发现和推理
zh.summary: 本论文通过结合事件序列建模、因果发现和大语言模型，解决车辆中的自动化故障诊断问题，处理具有数千个唯一代码的高维诊断序列。该框架引入了Transformer架构和多智能体系统用于错误模式规则的自动化合成。

fr.title: Apprendre à prédire, découvrir et raisonner dans les séquences d'événements discrètes de haute dimension
fr.summary: Cette thèse aborde le diagnostic automatisé des pannes dans les véhicules en combinant la modélisation des séquences d'événements, la découverte causale et les LLM pour traiter des séquences de diagnostic de haute dimension avec des milliers de codes uniques. Le cadre introduit des architectures Transformer et des systèmes multi-agents pour la synthèse automatisée des règles de modèles d'erreur.

de.title: Lernen zu Vorhersagen, Entdecken und Reasoning in hochdimensionalen diskreten Ereignissequenzen
de.summary: Diese These behandelt automatisierte Fehlerdiagnose in Fahrzeugen durch die Kombination von Ereignissequenzmodellierung, kausaler Entdeckung und LLMs zur Verarbeitung hochdimensionaler Diagnosesequenzen mit Tausenden eindeutiger Codes. Das Framework führt Transformer-Architekturen und Multi-Agent-Systeme für die automatisierte Synthese von Fehlermusters-Regeln ein.

es.title: Aprender a predecir, descubrir y razonar en secuencias de eventos discretos de alta dimensión
es.summary: Esta tesis aborda el diagnóstico automático de fallos en vehículos combinando modelado de secuencias de eventos, descubrimiento causal y LLMs para procesar secuencias de diagnóstico de alta dimensión con miles de códigos únicos. El marco introduce arquitecturas Transformer y sistemas multiagentes para la síntesis automatizada de reglas de patrones de error.

title: FactorEngine: A Program-level Knowledge-Infused Factor Mining Framework for Quantitative Investment
summary: FactorEngine proposes a program-level factor discovery framework that represents trading factors as executable code and improves effectiveness through separating logic revision from parameter optimization. The approach achieves state-of-the-art predictive performance and portfolio impact on real-world market data.
content:
arXiv:2603.16365v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We study alpha factor mining, the automated discovery of predictive signals from noisy, non-stationary market data-under a practical requirement that mined factors be directly executable and auditable, and that the discovery process remain computationally tractable at scale. Existing symbolic approaches are limited by bounded expressiveness, while neural forecasters often trade interpretability for performance and remain vulnerable to regime shifts and overfitting. We introduce FactorEngine (FE), a program-level factor discovery framework that casts factors as Turing-complete code and improves both effectiveness and efficiency via three separations: (i) logic revision vs. parameter optimization, (ii) LLM-guided directional search vs. Bayesian hyperparameter search, and (iii) LLM usage vs. local computation. FE further incorporates a knowledge-infused bootstrapping module that transforms unstructured financial reports into executable factor programs through a closed-loop multi-agent extraction-verification-code-generation pipeline, and an experience knowledge base that supports trajectory-aware refinement (including learning from failures). Across extensive backtests on real-world OHLCV data, FE produces factors with substantially stronger predictive stability and portfolio impact-for example, higher IC/ICIR (and Rank IC/ICIR) and improved AR/Sharpe, than baseline methods, achieving state-of-the-art predictive and portfolio performance.

zh.title: FactorEngine：用于量化投资的程序级知识注入因子挖掘框架
zh.summary: FactorEngine提出了一个程序级因子发现框架，将交易因子表示为可执行代码，并通过分离逻辑修订和参数优化来提高有效性。该方法在真实市场数据上实现了最先进的预测性能和投资组合影响。

fr.title: FactorEngine : un cadre d'extraction de facteurs enrichi par les connaissances au niveau du programme pour l'investissement quantitatif
fr.summary: FactorEngine propose un cadre de découverte de facteurs au niveau du programme qui représente les facteurs de trading en tant que code exécutable et améliore l'efficacité en séparant la révision logique de l'optimisation des paramètres. L'approche réalise des performances prédictives et un impact de portefeuille de pointe sur les données de marché du monde réel.

de.title: FactorEngine: Ein auf Programmebene wissensbereicherndes Faktoren-Mining-Framework für quantitative Investitionen
de.summary: FactorEngine schlägt ein Faktor-Entdeckungs-Framework auf Programmebene vor, das Handelsfaktoren als ausführbaren Code darstellt und die Effektivität durch die Trennung von Logik-Überprüfung und Parameteroptimierung verbessert. Der Ansatz erreicht hochmoderne Vorhersageleistung und Portfolioauswirkungen auf realen Marktdaten.

es.title: FactorEngine: Un marco de minería de factores enriquecido con conocimiento a nivel de programa para inversión cuantitativa
es.summary: FactorEngine propone un marco de descubrimiento de factores a nivel de programa que representa los factores comerciales como código ejecutable y mejora la efectividad separando la revisión de lógica de la optimización de parámetros. El enfoque logra un rendimiento predictivo de última generación y un impacto de cartera en datos de mercado del mundo real.

title: Via Negativa for AI Alignment: Why Negative Constraints Are Structurally Superior to Positive Preferences
summary: This theoretical paper explains why LLM alignment through negative-only feedback outperforms preference-based approaches, arguing that negative constraints encode discrete verifiable prohibitions while positive preferences encode ambiguous context-dependent values. The analysis recommends shifting alignment research toward learning what humans reject rather than prefer.
content:
arXiv:2603.16417v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent empirical results have demonstrated that training large language models (LLMs) with negative-only feedback can match or exceed standard reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). Negative Sample Reinforcement achieves parity with PPO on mathematical reasoning; Distributional Dispreference Optimization trains effectively using only dispreferred samples; and Constitutional AI outperforms pure RLHF on harmlessness benchmarks. Yet no unified theoretical account explains why negative signals are so effective. This paper proposes such an account: positive preferences and negative constraints are structurally asymmetric. Positive preferences ("which is better") encode continuously coupled, context-dependent human values that cannot be exhaustively specified -- leading models to learn surface correlates such as agreement with the user (sycophancy). Negative constraints ("what is wrong") encode discrete, finite, independently verifiable prohibitions that can converge to a stable boundary. This asymmetry -- rooted in Popper's falsification logic and the epistemology of negative knowledge -- explains both the sycophancy failure of preference-based RLHF and the surprising effectiveness of negative-signal methods. We argue that alignment research should shift its center of gravity from "learning what humans prefer" to "learning what humans reject," and offer testable predictions for this framework.

zh.title: Via Negativa用于AI对齐：为什么负面约束在结构上优于正面偏好
zh.summary: 这篇理论论文解释了为什么通过仅负面反馈的大语言模型对齐优于基于偏好的方法，论证负面约束编码离散可验证的禁止，而正面偏好编码模糊的上下文相关值。该分析建议将对齐研究转向学习人类拒绝的内容而不是偏好的内容。

fr.title: Via Negativa pour l'alignement de l'IA : Pourquoi les contraintes négatives sont structurellement supérieures aux préférences positives
fr.summary: Cet article théorique explique pourquoi l'alignement des LLM par retour négatif uniquement surpasse les approches basées sur les préférences, arguant que les contraintes négatives codent des interdictions discrètes vérifiables tandis que les préférences positives codent des valeurs contextuelles ambiguës. L'analyse recommande de réorienter la recherche en alignement vers l'apprentissage de ce que les humains rejettent plutôt que ce qu'ils préfèrent.

de.title: Via Negativa für KI-Ausrichtung: Warum negative Einschränkungen strukturell überlegen sind als positive Präferenzen
de.summary: Dieses theoretische Papier erklärt, warum die LLM-Ausrichtung durch nur negative Rückmeldung Präferenz-basierte Ansätze übertrifft, und argumentiert, dass negative Einschränkungen diskrete überprüfbare Verbote kodieren, während positive Präferenzen mehrdeutige kontextabhängige Werte kodieren. Die Analyse empfiehlt, die Ausrichtungsforschung darauf zu verlagern, zu lernen, was Menschen ablehnen, anstatt was sie bevorzugen.

es.title: Vía Negativa para alineación de IA: Por qué las restricciones negativas son estructuralmente superiores a las preferencias positivas
es.summary: Este artículo teórico explica por qué la alineación de LLM a través de retroalimentación solo negativa supera los enfoques basados en preferencias, argumentando que las restricciones negativas codifican prohibiciones discretas verificables mientras que las preferencias positivas codifican valores contextuales ambiguos. El análisis recomienda cambiar la investigación de alineación hacia aprender lo que los humanos rechazan en lugar de lo que prefieren.

title: From Natural Language to Executable Option Strategies via Large Language Models
summary: This work introduces Option Query Language (OQL), a domain-specific intermediate representation enabling LLMs to reliably translate natural-language trading intents into executable option strategies. The neuro-symbolic pipeline validates and executes queries deterministically, significantly improving accuracy over direct generation methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.16434v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at general code generation, yet translating natural-language trading intents into correct option strategies remains challenging. Real-world option design requires reasoning over massive, multi-dimensional option chain data with strict constraints, which often overwhelms direct generation methods. We introduce the Option Query Language (OQL), a domain-specific intermediate representation that abstracts option markets into high-level primitives under grammatical rules, enabling LLMs to function as reliable semantic parsers rather than free-form programmers. OQL queries are then validated and executed deterministically by an engine to instantiate executable strategies. We also present a new dataset for this task and demonstrate that our neuro-symbolic pipeline significantly improves execution accuracy and logical consistency over direct baselines.

zh.title: 从自然语言到可执行期权策略：通过大型语言模型
zh.summary: 这项工作引入了选项查询语言（OQL），一种特定领域的中间表示，使LLM能够可靠地将自然语言交易意图转化为可执行的期权策略。神经符号管道确定性地验证和执行查询，与直接生成方法相比显著提高了准确性。

fr.title: Du Langage Naturel aux Stratégies d'Options Exécutables via les Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit le Option Query Language (OQL), une représentation intermédiaire spécifique au domaine permettant aux LLM de traduire de manière fiable les intentions commerciales en langage naturel en stratégies d'options exécutables. Le pipeline neuro-symbolique valide et exécute les requêtes de manière déterministe, améliorant considérablement la précision par rapport aux méthodes de génération directe.

de.title: Von natürlicher Sprache zu ausführbaren Optionsstrategien über große Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt Option Query Language (OQL) ein, eine domänenspezifische Zwischendarstellung, die LLMs ermöglicht, natürlichsprachliche Handelsabsichten zuverlässig in ausführbare Optionsstrategien zu übersetzen. Die neurosymbolische Pipeline validiert und führt Abfragen deterministisch aus und verbessert die Genauigkeit gegenüber direkten Generierungsmethoden erheblich.

es.title: Del Lenguaje Natural a Estrategias de Opciones Ejecutables mediante Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce el Option Query Language (OQL), una representación intermedia específica del dominio que permite a los LLM traducir de manera confiable las intenciones comerciales en lenguaje natural en estrategias de opciones ejecutables. La canalización neurosimbólica valida y ejecuta consultas de manera determinista, mejorando significativamente la precisión en comparación con los métodos de generación directa.

title: Visual Distraction Undermines Moral Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
summary: This research reveals that visual inputs fundamentally compromise moral reasoning in state-of-the-art VLMs, bypassing text-based safety mechanisms by activating intuitive decision pathways. The Moral Dilemma Simulation benchmark demonstrates this fragility in multimodal safety alignment, exposing critical gaps in current safety techniques.
content:
arXiv:2603.16445v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Moral reasoning is fundamental to safe Artificial Intelligence (AI), yet ensuring its consistency across modalities becomes critical as AI systems evolve from text-based assistants to embodied agents. Current safety techniques demonstrate success in textual contexts, but concerns remain about generalization to visual inputs. Existing moral evaluation benchmarks rely on textonly formats and lack systematic control over variables that influence moral decision-making. Here we show that visual inputs fundamentally alter moral decision-making in state-of-the-art (SOTA) Vision-Language Models (VLMs), bypassing text-based safety mechanisms. We introduce Moral Dilemma Simulation (MDS), a multimodal benchmark grounded in Moral Foundation Theory (MFT) that enables mechanistic analysis through orthogonal manipulation of visual and contextual variables. The evaluation reveals that the vision modality activates intuition-like pathways that override the more deliberate and safer reasoning patterns observed in text-only contexts. These findings expose critical fragilities where language-tuned safety filters fail to constrain visual processing, demonstrating the urgent need for multimodal safety alignment.

zh.title: 视觉干扰破坏视觉-语言模型中的道德推理
zh.summary: 这项研究揭示了视觉输入从根本上破坏了最先进VLM中的道德推理，通过激活直觉决策途径绕过了基于文本的安全机制。道德困境模拟基准演示了多模态安全对齐中的脆弱性，暴露了当前安全技术中的关键漏洞。

fr.title: La Distraction Visuelle Compromet le Raisonnement Moral dans les Modèles Vision-Langage
fr.summary: Cette recherche révèle que les entrées visuelles compromettent fondamentalement le raisonnement moral dans les VLM de pointe, contournant les mécanismes de sécurité basés sur le texte en activant les voies décisionnelles intuitives. L'indice de simulation de dilemme moral démontre cette fragilité dans l'alignement de sécurité multimodale, exposant des lacunes critiques dans les techniques de sécurité actuelles.

de.title: Visuelle Ablenkung beeinträchtigt moralisches Denken in Vision-Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Diese Forschung zeigt, dass visuelle Eingaben das moralische Denken in hochmodernen VLMs grundlegend beeinträchtigen und textbasierte Sicherheitsmechanismen umgehen, indem sie intuitive Entscheidungswege aktivieren. Der Moral Dilemma Simulation-Benchmark demonstriert diese Zerbrechlichkeit bei der multimodalen Sicherheitsausrichtung und offenbart kritische Lücken in aktuellen Sicherheitstechniken.

es.title: La Distracción Visual Socava el Razonamiento Moral en los Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje
es.summary: Esta investigación revela que las entradas visuales comprenden fundamentalmente el razonamiento moral en los VLM de última generación, eludiendo los mecanismos de seguridad basados en texto al activar caminos de decisión intuitivos. El punto de referencia de simulación de dilema moral demuestra esta fragilidad en la alineación de seguridad multimodal, exponiendo brechas críticas en las técnicas de seguridad actuales.

title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas
summary: TRUST-SQL addresses Text-to-SQL parsing in real-world settings where databases contain hundreds of noisy tables requiring active schema discovery. The framework uses a four-phase protocol and dual-track GRPO strategy, achieving 30.6% relative improvement over baselines while operating without pre-loaded metadata.
content:
arXiv:2603.16448v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-to-SQL parsing has achieved remarkable progress under the Full Schema Assumption. However, this premise fails in real-world enterprise environments where databases contain hundreds of tables with massive noisy metadata. Rather than injecting the full schema upfront, an agent must actively identify and verify only the relevant subset, giving rise to the Unknown Schema scenario we study in this work. To address this, we propose TRUST-SQL (Truthful Reasoning with Unknown Schema via Tools). We formulate the task as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process where our autonomous agent employs a structured four-phase protocol to ground reasoning in verified metadata. Crucially, this protocol provides a structural boundary for our novel Dual-Track GRPO strategy. By applying token-level masked advantages, this strategy isolates exploration rewards from execution outcomes to resolve credit assignment, yielding a 9.9% relative improvement over standard GRPO. Extensive experiments across five benchmarks demonstrate that TRUST-SQL achieves an average absolute improvement of 30.6% and 16.6% for the 4B and 8B variants respectively over their base models. Remarkably, despite operating entirely without pre-loaded metadata, our framework consistently matches or surpasses strong baselines that rely on schema prefilling.

zh.title: TRUST-SQL：用于未知架构的工具集成多轮强化学习的文本到SQL
zh.summary: TRUST-SQL解决了在现实世界设置中的文本到SQL解析问题，其中数据库包含数百个需要主动架构发现的噪声表。该框架使用四阶段协议和双轨GRPO策略，在没有预加载元数据的情况下实现了相对于基线的30.6%相对改进。

fr.title: TRUST-SQL : Apprentissage par Renforcement Multi-Tours Intégré aux Outils pour Texte-to-SQL sur Schémas Inconnus
fr.summary: TRUST-SQL aborde l'analyse du Texte-to-SQL dans des paramètres du monde réel où les bases de données contiennent des centaines de tableaux bruyants nécessitant une découverte active du schéma. Le cadre utilise un protocole en quatre phases et une stratégie GRPO à double voie, réalisant une amélioration relative de 30,6% par rapport aux références tout en fonctionnant sans métadonnées préchargées.

de.title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integriertes Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning für Text-to-SQL über unbekannte Schemata
de.summary: TRUST-SQL behandelt die Text-to-SQL-Analyse in realen Umgebungen, in denen Datenbanken Hunderte von verrauschten Tabellen enthalten, die aktive Schemaermittlung erfordern. Das Framework verwendet ein vierphasiges Protokoll und eine Zwei-Spuren-GRPO-Strategie und erreicht eine relative Verbesserung von 30,6% gegenüber Baselines bei Betrieb ohne vorgeladene Metadaten.

es.title: TRUST-SQL: Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Multi-Turno Integrado con Herramientas para Texto a SQL en Esquemas Desconocidos
es.summary: TRUST-SQL aborda el análisis de texto a SQL en entornos del mundo real donde las bases de datos contienen cientos de tablas ruidosas que requieren descubrimiento activo de esquemas. El marco utiliza un protocolo de cuatro fases y una estrategia GRPO de doble vía, logrando una mejora relativa del 30,6% sobre las líneas base mientras funciona sin metadatos precargados.

title: RetailBench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Autonomous Decision-Making and Strategy Stability of LLM Agents in Realistic Retail Environments
summary: RetailBench introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLM-based agents on long-horizon decision-making in dynamic retail scenarios with stochastic demand and evolving conditions. The Evolving Strategy & Execution framework improves stability but reveals fundamental limitations of current LLMs on complex multi-factor decisions.
content:
arXiv:2603.16453v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents have achieved notable success on short-horizon and highly structured tasks. However, their ability to maintain coherent decision-making over long horizons in realistic and dynamic environments remains an open challenge.
  We introduce RetailBench, a high-fidelity benchmark designed to evaluate long-horizon autonomous decision-making in realistic commercial scenarios, where agents must operate under stochastic demand and evolving external conditions.
  We further propose the Evolving Strategy & Execution framework, which separates high-level strategic reasoning from low-level action execution. This design enables adaptive and interpretable strategy evolution over time. It is particularly important for long-horizon tasks, where non-stationary environments and error accumulation require strategies to be revised at a different temporal scale than action execution.
  Experiments on eight state-of-the-art LLMs across progressively challenging environments show that our framework improves operational stability and efficiency compared to other baselines. However, performance degrades substantially as task complexity increases, revealing fundamental limitations in current LLMs for long-horizon, multi-factor decision-making.

zh.title: RetailBench：评估大型语言模型代理在逼真零售环境中的长期自主决策和策略稳定性
zh.summary: RetailBench引入了一个基准，用于评估基于大型语言模型的代理在具有随机需求和不断变化条件的动态零售场景中的长期决策能力。进化策略与执行框架提高了稳定性，但揭示了当前大型语言模型在复杂多因素决策中的根本局限性。

fr.title: RetailBench : Évaluation de la prise de décision autonome à long horizon et de la stabilité stratégique des agents LLM dans des environnements de détail réalistes
fr.summary: RetailBench introduit un benchmark pour évaluer les agents basés sur les LLM dans la prise de décision à long horizon dans des scénarios de détail dynamiques avec une demande stochastique et des conditions évolutives. Le cadre Strategy & Execution évolutif améliore la stabilité mais révèle les limitations fondamentales des LLM actuels dans les décisions multi-facteurs complexes.

de.title: RetailBench: Bewertung der langfristigen autonomen Entscheidungsfindung und Strategiestabilität von LLM-Agenten in realistischen Einzelhandelsmilieus
de.summary: RetailBench führt einen Benchmark zur Bewertung von LLM-basierten Agenten bei der langfristigen Entscheidungsfindung in dynamischen Einzelhandelsszenarios mit stochastischer Nachfrage und sich entwickelnden Bedingungen ein. Das evolvierende Strategy-&-Execution-Framework verbessert die Stabilität, offenbart aber grundlegende Einschränkungen aktueller LLMs bei komplexen multifaktoriellen Entscheidungen.

es.title: RetailBench: Evaluación de la toma de decisiones autónoma a largo plazo y la estabilidad estratégica de agentes LLM en entornos minoristas realistas
es.summary: RetailBench introduce un punto de referencia para evaluar agentes basados en LLM en la toma de decisiones a largo plazo en escenarios minoristas dinámicos con demanda estocástica y condiciones en evolución. El marco Strategy & Execution evolutivo mejora la estabilidad pero revela limitaciones fundamentales de los LLM actuales en decisiones complejas multifactoriales.

title: Follow the Clues, Frame the Truth: Hybrid-evidential Deductive Reasoning in Open-Vocabulary Multimodal Emotion Recognition
summary: HyDRA proposes a hybrid-evidential deductive reasoning architecture for open-vocabulary multimodal emotion recognition using a propose-verify-decide protocol. Hierarchical reinforcement learning aligns reasoning trajectories with task performance, consistently outperforming baselines especially in ambiguous and conflicting scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.16463v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Open-Vocabulary Multimodal Emotion Recognition (OV-MER) is inherently challenging due to the ambiguity of equivocal multimodal cues, which often stem from distinct unobserved situational dynamics. While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) offer extensive semantic coverage, their performance is often bottlenecked by premature commitment to dominant data priors, resulting in suboptimal heuristics that overlook crucial, complementary affective cues across modalities. We argue that effective affective reasoning requires more than surface-level association; it necessitates reconstructing nuanced emotional states by synthesizing multiple evidence-grounded rationales that reconcile these observations from diverse latent perspectives. We introduce HyDRA, a Hybrid-evidential Deductive Reasoning Architecture that formalizes inference as a Propose-Verify-Decide protocol. To internalize this abductive process, we employ reinforcement learning with hierarchical reward shaping, aligning the reasoning trajectories with final task performance to ensure they best reconcile the observed multimodal cues. Systematic evaluations validate our design choices, with HyDRA consistently outperforming strong baselines--especially in ambiguous or conflicting scenarios--while providing interpretable, diagnostic evidence traces.

zh.title: 跟随线索，框架真理：开放词汇多模态情感识别中的混合证据演绎推理
zh.summary: HyDRA为开放词汇多模态情感识别提出了一种混合证据演绎推理架构，使用提议-验证-决定协议。分层强化学习将推理轨迹与任务性能对齐，特别是在模糊和冲突场景中始终优于基准。

fr.title: Suivez les indices, encadrez la vérité : Raisonnement déductif hybride-évidentiel en reconnaissance d'émotion multimodale à vocabulaire ouvert
fr.summary: HyDRA propose une architecture de raisonnement déductif hybride-évidentiel pour la reconnaissance d'émotion multimodale à vocabulaire ouvert utilisant un protocole propose-verify-decide. L'apprentissage par renforcement hiérarchique aligne les trajectoires de raisonnement avec les performances de la tâche, surpassant systématiquement les lignes de base, en particulier dans les scénarios ambigus et conflictuels.

de.title: Folgen Sie den Hinweisen, rahmen Sie die Wahrheit: Hybrid-evidential deduktives Denken bei offener Vokabular-Multimodal-Emotionserkennung
de.summary: HyDRA schlägt eine hybrid-evidential deduktive Reasoning-Architektur für offene Vokabular-Multimodal-Emotionserkennung vor, die ein Propose-Verify-Decide-Protokoll verwendet. Hierarchisches Reinforcement Learning richtet Reasoning-Trajektorien an Task-Leistung aus und übertrifft Baselines konsistent, besonders in mehrdeutigen und konfliktären Szenarien.

es.title: Siga las pistas, enmarque la verdad: Razonamiento deductivo híbrido-evidente en reconocimiento de emociones multimodales de vocabulario abierto
es.summary: HyDRA propone una arquitectura de razonamiento deductivo híbrido-evidente para el reconocimiento de emociones multimodales de vocabulario abierto utilizando un protocolo de proponer-verificar-decidir. El aprendizaje por refuerzo jerárquico alinea las trayectorias de razonamiento con el desempeño de la tarea, superando consistentemente los baselines especialmente en escenarios ambiguos y conflictivos.

title: Breaking the Chain: A Causal Analysis of LLM Faithfulness to Intermediate Structures
summary: This causal evaluation study finds that LLMs appear self-consistent with intermediate structures but fail to update outputs when those structures are edited in up to 60% of cases. The research reveals intermediate structures function as influential context rather than stable causal determinants of model outputs.
content:
arXiv:2603.16475v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Schema-guided reasoning pipelines ask LLMs to produce explicit intermediate structures -- rubrics, checklists, verification queries -- before committing to a final decision. But do these structures causally determine the output, or merely accompany it? We introduce a causal evaluation protocol that makes this directly measurable: by selecting tasks where a deterministic function maps intermediate structures to decisions, every controlled edit implies a unique correct output. Across eight models and three benchmarks, models appear self-consistent with their own intermediate structures but fail to update predictions after intervention in up to 60% of cases -- revealing that apparent faithfulness is fragile once the intermediate structure changes. When derivation of the final decision from the structure is delegated to an external tool, this fragility largely disappears; however, prompts which ask to prioritize the intermediate structure over the original input do not materially close the gap. Overall, intermediate structures in schema-guided pipelines function as influential context rather than stable causal mediators.

zh.title: 打破链条：大型语言模型对中间结构的忠实性因果分析
zh.summary: 这项因果评估研究发现，大型语言模型似乎与中间结构自我一致，但在这些结构被编辑时，在高达60%的情况下无法更新输出。该研究揭示中间结构充当有影响力的上下文，而不是模型输出的稳定因果决定因素。

fr.title: Casser la chaîne : une analyse causale de la fidélité des LLM aux structures intermédiaires
fr.summary: Cette étude d'évaluation causale découvre que les LLM semblent auto-cohérents avec les structures intermédiaires mais ne mettent pas à jour les résultats lorsque ces structures sont modifiées dans jusqu'à 60% des cas. La recherche révèle que les structures intermédiaires fonctionnent comme un contexte influent plutôt que des déterminants causaux stables des résultats du modèle.

de.title: Die Kette durchbrechen: Eine kausale Analyse der LLM-Treue zu Zwischenstrukturen
de.summary: Diese kausale Evaluierungsstudie stellt fest, dass LLMs self-konsistent mit Zwischenstrukturen erscheinen, aber in bis zu 60% der Fälle Ausgaben nicht aktualisieren, wenn diese Strukturen bearbeitet werden. Die Forschung zeigt, dass Zwischenstrukturen als einflussreicher Kontext fungieren, nicht als stabile kausale Determinanten von Modellausgaben.

es.title: Romper la cadena: un análisis causal de la fidelidad de LLM a estructuras intermedias
es.summary: Este estudio de evaluación causal encuentra que los LLM parecen auto-consistentes con estructuras intermedias pero no actualizan las salidas cuando esas estructuras se editan en hasta el 60% de los casos. La investigación revela que las estructuras intermedias funcionan como contexto influyente en lugar de determinantes causales estables de las salidas del modelo.
